summary: Read-only parallel audit of the Kenshi-Online multiplayer mod: map every subsystem, find the broken sync-loop gaps, propose clean reorg

synthesis.executiveSummary: The sync loop is bugged today because it is mathematically deadlocked at the "game loaded" gate, not because of any networking or protocol flaw. ENet connects, handshakes, and streams 4,900+ packets reliably, but the client never transitions out of ClientPhase::Connected/GameReady because OnGameLoaded() is triggered almost exclusively by the CharacterCreate hook's loading-burst detector (core.cpp:1346-1362: needs GetLoadingCreateCount()>0 then 3s quiet). At init, GameWorldSingleton resolved UNRESOLVED and ~30 patterns failed, so the global-pointer fallback (core.cpp:1381, gated on playerBaseValid||gameWorldValid) also cannot fire. With the gate stuck closed, packet_handler.cpp:576-580 drops every S2C_EntitySpawn (0x31) with a TODO-"queue for later" that was never implemented, and the deferred-spawn queue is never drained. So remote characters never appear — not even via the fragile NPC-hijack/squad-bypass fallback, which itself sits downstream of the same gate. On top of this single fatal blocker sit a pile of half-built and duplicate subsystems (three spawn mechanisms, two position pipelines, an SDK abstraction that is initialized but never queried, four to seven overlapping "orchestrators," combat that only syncs death/KO because C2S_AttackIntent is never sent, and a server with a HandlePlayerReady that references a non-existent struct and won't compile), which collectively make the data flow impossible to reason about. The good news: the network, protocol, registry, interpolation, hooking/MovRaxRsp infrastructure, and UI are genuinely solid and salvageable.

topGapsBlockingFullLoop.gap: Client never reaches GameLoaded state; every entity spawn (0x31) is dropped, never queued/replayed. The full sync loop cannot start.
topGapsBlockingFullLoop.rootCause: OnGameLoaded() is gated on the CharacterCreate loading-burst detector (core.cpp:1346-1362) OR a global-pointer fallback (core.cpp:1381) that requires playerBaseValid||gameWorldValid. At runtime GameWorldSingleton was UNRESOLVED and PlayerBase failed, so neither path fires. packet_handler.cpp:576-580 drops spawns with an unimplemented 'TODO: Queue for later spawn'.
topGapsBlockingFullLoop.fixApproach: (1) Make game-load detection robust and source-independent: poll CharacterIterator count directly each tick as the PRIMARY signal (it already works as a fallback at core.cpp:1382) rather than depending on the CharacterCreate hook or unresolved globals. Trigger OnGameLoaded() the moment iterator count>0 and stable for ~1-2s. (2) Implement the missing deferred-spawn queue: when a spawn arrives before GameReady, push it into a real buffer and replay the whole buffer in OnGameLoaded(). Apply the same to all state messages (position/health) keyed by entity. (3) Add a hard time-based ultimate fallback that does NOT require any global pointer.

topGapsBlockingFullLoop.gap: Critical game-function resolution is unreliable: GameWorldSingleton + ~30 patterns unresolved, SaveGame/LoadGame nulled on signature mismatch, ApplyDamage deliberately skipped.
topGapsBlockingFullLoop.rootCause: Pattern scanner's FindGlobalNearString uses a 512B-4KB window inadequate for 6-9KB functions (patterns.cpp:65-75); FindFunctionStart fragility; signature-mismatch nulling. These feed the load gate and combat directly.
topGapsBlockingFullLoop.fixApproach: Harden global-pointer discovery (extend scan window to 8KB using .pdata bounds), and crucially decouple the game-loaded decision from these globals (see gap 1) so resolution failures degrade gracefully instead of deadlocking. Verify GameWorldSingleton offset against current Steam binary.

topGapsBlockingFullLoop.gap: Server HandlePlayerReady does not compile / references non-existent struct, breaking the ready handshake that gates spawning.
topGapsBlockingFullLoop.rootCause: server.cpp:2405-2451 implements against an undefined 'Player&' type and calls BroadcastReliable()/fields (.connected) that don't exist on ConnectedPlayer (server.h:17-29, declared as ConnectedPlayer&).
topGapsBlockingFullLoop.fixApproach: Rewrite HandlePlayerReady to take ConnectedPlayer&, iterate m_players using real fields (lobbyReady/isReady), and use existing broadcast helpers. Add a single authoritative ready/lobby state machine on the server.

topGapsBlockingFullLoop.gap: Lobby coordination is missing: clients never auto-send C2S_LobbyReady, so there is no 'all players loaded before first spawn' barrier.
topGapsBlockingFullLoop.rootCause: C2S_LobbyReady is only sent via manual /ready command (builtin_commands.cpp:1770); HandleAllPlayersReady is stubbed (packet_handler.cpp:1773). Faction assignment never closes the lobby phase.
topGapsBlockingFullLoop.fixApproach: Auto-send C2S_LobbyReady once faction is patched + game is loaded; server waits for all, broadcasts S2C_LobbyStart; clients then transition to spawning. Collapse scattered ready flags into one client-side phase enum.

topGapsBlockingFullLoop.gap: Remote character spawn pipeline is non-deterministic and fragile (depends on game spawning a nearby NPC to hijack).
topGapsBlockingFullLoop.rootCause: Primary path is in-place NPC hijack via Hook_CharacterCreate (entity_hooks.cpp:918-954) + squad-spawn flag flipping; direct FactoryCreate is only a 15s fallback. In safe zones/interiors no NPC is created, so nothing fires.
topGapsBlockingFullLoop.fixApproach: Make FactoryCreate-with-mod-template the PRIMARY, deterministic path (mod ships Player 1-16 CHARACTER templates with stable factions). Delete NPC-hijack and squad bypass, or keep only as a last resort. Add spawn timeout + cap-slot release on disconnect (fixes the cap-saturation bug at sync_orchestrator.cpp:797).

topGapsBlockingFullLoop.gap: Cross-player combat only syncs death/KO, not damage; intermediate health invisible; server combat resolver is dead.
topGapsBlockingFullLoop.rootCause: C2S_AttackIntent is never sent (ApplyDamage hook disabled for crash risk, combat_hooks.cpp:270-278), so server ResolveCombat()/HandleAttackIntent are unreachable. C2S_LimbHealth only piggybacks death/KO.
topGapsBlockingFullLoop.fixApproach: Choose ONE model: add a periodic remote-health poll (e.g. 200-500ms) so limb/health states stream continuously, and keep death/KO event hooks authoritative for end-states. Delete the unreachable server-side ResolveCombat path unless attack intent is actually wired.

topGapsBlockingFullLoop.gap: Equipment/appearance never applied to remote characters; they spawn with hijacked-NPC gear; faction write disabled.
topGapsBlockingFullLoop.rootCause: S2C_EntitySpawn carries no equipment payload (server.cpp:1181); SetEquipment() requires the item already in inventory (game_inventory.cpp:129-179); faction write on remotes disabled due to use-after-free (player_controller.cpp:159-167).
topGapsBlockingFullLoop.fixApproach: Include an appearance/equipment block in the spawn payload; on the client, materialize items into inventory then equip. For faction, replicate a server-assigned player faction id per entity and have the client resolve it to a persistent local faction (mod-template faction) instead of writing captured NPC pointers.

topGapsBlockingFullLoop.gap: Two position pipelines and an unused SDK run simultaneously, causing double-sends and unclear data flow.
topGapsBlockingFullLoop.rootCause: Legacy PollLocalPositions/ApplyRemotePositionsDirect (core.cpp:2879-3039) and SyncOrchestrator stages both active under a feature flag; movement hooks also send when resolved. KenshiSDK snapshot/diff methods have zero callers.
topGapsBlockingFullLoop.fixApproach: Pick SyncOrchestrator as the single driver, delete the legacy core.cpp position path, and either route reads through the SDK or delete the SDK's unused query surface. Position write Method 3 (cached field) drifts — restrict to Methods 1/2.

synthesis.orchestratorVerdict: Keep SyncOrchestrator as THE single sync driver (rename to SyncPipeline — it is the pipeline, it does not orchestrate other orchestrators). It is the only component actively ticked every frame that owns the real read→send→interpolate→write loop and its sub-engines (EntityResolver, ZoneEngine, PlayerEngine). DELETE or demote everything overlapping it: (1) DELETE the legacy in-core sync path (PollLocalPositions / ApplyRemotePositionsDirect / SendCachedPackets in core.cpp) — it duplicates SyncOrchestrator stages and causes double-sends. (2) DELETE zone_interest.cpp (ZoneInterestManager singleton) — dead clone of ZoneEngine, never called. (3) DEMOTE PipelineOrchestrator to a diagnostics module (rename PipelineMonitor, move to sys/diagnostics) — it is 1Hz telemetry, not sync; keep but clearly outside the sync path. (4) DELETE SyncFacilitator and OwnershipManager — pure facades that delegate straight back to the registry/engines; callers should hit those directly. (5) KEEP LoadingOrchestrator but reduce it to one job: game-load detection + spawn gating, feeding the single SyncPipeline (and fix it to not depend on unresolved globals). (6) PatternOrchestrator (Scanner) is unrelated (pattern discovery) — keep, it is not a sync orchestrator. (7) TaskOrchestrator (UI/worker threads) is unrelated — keep. Net result: ONE sync driver (SyncPipeline), ONE load/spawn gate (LoadingOrchestrator), ONE diagnostics tap (PipelineMonitor), and no facades.

proposedArchitecture.responsibility: Wire format: message enums, packed structs with EXPLICIT variable-length payload schemas (spawn appearance/equipment/health inline), channel assignment, version. Remove dead S2C_ZoneData/C2S_EntityAck; dedicate combat-stance msg; bounds-checked string read.
ENet lifecycle, channelized send/recv, keepalive using the constant, adaptive session timeout set BEFORE connect returns (fixes timeout cliff).
Decode S2C, route to subsystem appliers, and queue-while-not-ready (real deferred buffer replayed on GameLoaded) with per-entity locking on snapshot writes.
Authoritative net-id<->game-object map, ownership, generation field (actually implemented), spawn-state with timeout cleanup.
Per-entity 8-snapshot ring buffer, jitter-adaptive delay, slerp/lerp, snap correction. Skip entities with no game object.
THE single per-tick driver: poll+send local positions, drive interpolation, write remote positions, poll+send equipment/health diffs, drive zone interest. Owns EntityResolver/ZoneEngine/PlayerEngine.
proposedArchitecture.builtFrom: KenshiMP.Core/sync/sync_orchestrator.cpp — keep, rename, absorb legacy core path

proposedArchitecture.responsibility: Detect game-loaded via CharacterIterator polling (not unresolved globals), drain deferred spawn buffer, gate first spawn, run lobby ready barrier.
Deterministic remote-char creation via FactoryCreate + mod Player templates; per-player cap with disconnect release; spawn timeout. NPC-hijack only as optional last resort.
proposedArchitecture.builtFrom: merge spawn_manager.cpp + entity_hooks spawn path; delete asset_facilitator/squad_spawn_hooks

proposedArchitecture.responsibility: Apply equipment/appearance/faction to spawned remote chars from spawn payload; faction via server-assigned id resolved to persistent local faction (no NPC-pointer writes).
Death/KO event hooks (authoritative end-states) + periodic remote health/limb poll for intermediate damage. One model, server validates.
proposedArchitecture.builtFrom: KenshiMP.Core/hooks/combat_hooks.cpp — keep events, add health poll; delete dead server ResolveCombat path

proposedArchitecture.responsibility: Single low-level read/write primitive layer (CharacterAccessor/iterator/offsets). One access pattern, no duplicate WorldAccessor.
proposedArchitecture.builtFrom: KenshiMP.Core/game/game_types.h + sdk/kenshi_sdk.cpp — consolidate, drop unused query surface or wire it as the source of truth

proposedArchitecture.responsibility: Pattern scan, .pdata function bounds, MinHook + MovRaxRsp detour, global-pointer discovery with 8KB window.
Per-tick authoritative hub: validated position/combat/state, entity ids, lobby+ready state machine, broadcast, JSON persistence.
proposedArchitecture.builtFrom: KenshiMP.Server/server.cpp — keep, fix HandlePlayerReady compile + ready FSM

proposedArchitecture.responsibility: MyGUI HUD, menu, chat, slash commands, connection state machine (explicit enum).
1Hz telemetry/anomaly snapshots, OUTSIDE the sync path.
