mvpSequence: Fix server compile: rewrite GameServer::HandlePlayerReady (server.cpp:2405-2451) to use ConnectedPlayer& and real fields; add one authoritative lobby/ready state machine. Without this the server build is broken.

Break the load deadlock: make LoadGate trigger OnGameLoaded() from CharacterIterator polling (already proven as fallback at core.cpp:1382) instead of depending on the CharacterCreate burst detector or unresolved GameWorldSingleton/PlayerBase. Add a time-based ultimate fallback with no global-pointer dependency.

Implement the real deferred-spawn buffer in PacketRouter: replace the TODO at packet_handler.cpp:579 with an actual queue; replay ALL buffered spawns (and queued position/health) in OnGameLoaded(). Verify a remote S2C_EntitySpawn is no longer dropped.

Pick ONE sync driver: enable SyncOrchestrator unconditionally, delete the legacy core.cpp position path, delete zone_interest/OwnershipManager/SyncFacilitator/asset_facilitator. Confirm exactly one read→send and one write per entity per tick.

Make spawning deterministic: route SpawnService to FactoryCreate using mod Player templates as the primary path; remove dependence on NPC hijack. Add spawn timeout + cap-slot release on disconnect. Goal: remote char reliably appears within ~1-2s of join.
Wire the lobby barrier: auto-send C2S_LobbyReady after faction patch + game loaded; server broadcasts S2C_LobbyStart; clients spawn only after barrier. Both players see each other before first spawn.

Verify the 2-player vertical slice: two clients connect, both reach GameLoaded, exchange C2S/S2C_PositionUpdate, each spawns the other's character via FactoryCreate, positions interpolate smoothly. This is the working slice.

Layer in continuous remote health/limb poll (200-500ms) + keep death/KO event hooks so cross-player combat shows intermediate damage and consistent kills. Delete the unreachable server ResolveCombat path.

Add appearance/equipment to the spawn payload and apply on the client (materialize-then-equip); resolve faction via server-assigned id to a persistent local faction. Remotes now look correct and are non-hostile.
Defer/strip non-MVP: building sync, master-server polish, packet compression, endianness — explicitly out of the vertical slice.

rawFindings.whatItActuallyDoes: Defines a 58-message multiplayer protocol with 3 ENet channels (reliable ordered, reliable unordered, unreliable sequenced). Implements message types for connection handshake, entity spawn/despawn, position updates, combat hits/death/KO, stats/health/equipment, inventory/trade, buildings, squads, factions, chat, admin, and server queries. Uses fixed-size packed structures with variable-length string payloads (uint16_t length prefix). Includes Quat smallest-three compression (32 bits) and float16 position delta codecs, but these compression utilities are defined but never used. Protocol version is hardcoded to 1; no backwards compatibility path exists.
rawFindings.wiringStatus: Core messages are wired and functioning: C2S/S2C_Handshake, C2S/S2C_PositionUpdate, S2C_CombatHit/Death/KO, S2C_EntitySpawn/Despawn, S2C_HostAssignment, C2S_ChatMessage, S2C_HealthUpdate, S2C_EquipmentUpdate, S2C_LimbHealth, S2C_StatusEffect, S2C_BuildPlaced/Destroyed/Progress, S2C_SquadCreated/MemberUpdate, S2C_FactionRelation, Master Server MS_Register/Heartbeat/QueryList, and Pipeline Debug messages all have callers in server.cpp and handlers in packet_handler.cpp. DEAD: S2C_ZoneData (0x12) and C2S_EntityAck (0x15) are defined but never sent or handled anywhere. Compression utilities (DeltaPosition, PackedVelocity, float16 codecs) are defined in compression.h but instantiated nowhere—actual CharacterPosition struct uses full float32 positions.

brokenForSync.symptom: Message type aliasing creates ambiguity
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: S2C_CombatBlock (0x42) reused to carry MsgCombatStance data instead of using a dedicated message type

brokenForSync.symptom: Variable-length payloads lack schema definition
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: MsgEntitySpawn, MsgChatMessage, MsgSquadCreate declare data but struct is incomplete; real encoding (name, equipment, health) documented only in comments, not in type definition

brokenForSync.symptom: PacketHeader.flags compression bit never used
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: WriteHeader() always sets flags=0; no packet compression despite 30-40% potential bandwidth savings
brokenForSync.location: protocol.h:145-151 (WriteHeader never sets bit 0); server.cpp (1397-1434 Broadcast functions never compress)

brokenForSync.symptom: Hardcoded string length limit vs protocol capacity mismatch
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: ReadString() has 1024 char hardcoded maxLen, but struct uses uint16_t length (capacity 65535). Chat messages silently truncated without error reporting.

brokenForSync.symptom: No endianness conversion for network byte order
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: All structures use native byte order (little-endian x86). htons/ntohl never called. Fails on big-endian systems.
brokenForSync.location: All protocol.h, messages.h files — no byte-order conversions anywhere

brokenForSync.symptom: Input validation gaps in message handlers
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: MsgStatUpdate not bounds-checked for stat index 0-23; MsgEntitySpawn doesn't validate templateId; MsgCombatHit relies on entity lookup to catch missing entities

rawFindings.reorgNote: KEEP: The protocol foundation is sound—channel assignments are correct, core messages are wired and working, serialization is fundamentally correct. Version checking exists. REFACTOR: (1) Remove dead S2C_ZoneData and C2S_EntityAck enums. (2) Formalize variable-length payload schemas—define explicit structs for MsgEntitySpawn extended fields (health, alive flag) and make string payloads explicit in the struct. (3) Activate packet compression: set PacketHeader.flags bit 0 for messages larger than 128 bytes; implement lightweight payload compression (even simple zlib or LZ4 achieves 30%+ savings). (4) Add string length validation: replace hardcoded 1024 limit with proper bounds checks aligned to uint16_t capacity. (5) Dedicate S2C_CombatStance as its own message type (reuse 0x0C or nearby) instead of aliasing S2C_CombatBlock. (6) Add endianness safety: wrap all multi-byte fields with platform-portable conversions (htons-style) or document x86-only assumption. (7) Tighten input validation: stat index bounds, templateId existence, entity lookup failures. This is NOT a "delete and rewrite"—the protocol scales and is extensible. These are maturation refinements for 1.0 shipping.

rawFindings.subsystem: KenshiMP.Core/net - Client-side network layer (connection, messaging, dispatching)
rawFindings.whatItActuallyDoes: The net subsystem manages the complete client-server lifecycle: ENet connection initialization (blocking and async modes), message packet reading/writing, dispatch to game-world handlers via PacketHandler, and keepalive/disconnect management. NetworkClient (client.h/cpp) wraps ENet with a callback-based receive system and separate threadsafe send methods per channel (reliable ordered, reliable unordered, unreliable sequenced). PacketHandler (packet_handler.cpp) decodes 40+ S2C message types and applies state to game world or entity registry, with game-thread queueing for unsafe operations. ServerQueryClient (server_query.cpp) implements separate lightweight server browser queries without blocking gameplay.
rawFindings.wiringStatus: Fully wired in live path: NetworkClient.Update() called every ~1ms from Core::NetworkThreadFunc (core.cpp:1789), which pumps ENet events and triggers PacketCallback set at initialization (packet_handler.cpp:114). Handshake → EntitySpawn → PositionUpdate → combat/state sync forms the main player-visible loop. Keepalive sent every 5s from OnGameTick (core.cpp:2131-2135). ServerQueryClient is optional (not blocking game).

brokenForSync.symptom: S2C_ZoneData and S2C_ServerInfo defined in protocol but never handled in PacketHandler
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: Message types appear in protocol.h (0x12, 0xA1) but no case statement or Handle* function exists — will fall through to unknown message log (line 330) and be silently dropped

brokenForSync.symptom: Keepalive interval is 5 seconds in code but KMP_KEEPALIVE_INTERVAL constant is 1000ms — semantic mismatch
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: core.cpp line 2131 hardcodes `>= 5` seconds while constants.h defines KMP_KEEPALIVE_INTERVAL=1000. Code doesn't use the constant, so timeout tuning is fragile

brokenForSync.symptom: Async connect timeout (5s) set during ConnectAsync but session timeout bumped to 30-60s only AFTER connection established — during load/gameplay with no position updates for 5+ seconds, client gets booted
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: enet_peer_timeout called at line 86 with 5s, then changed to 30-60s at line 141. Between those points (when game is loading or player standing still with no position sends), server may timeout and disconnect

brokenForSync.symptom: S2C_HandshakeAck handler calls entity_hooks::ResumeForNetwork() only if game already loaded (line 366), defers to OnGameLoaded if not — but OnGameLoaded isn't called immediately after connect if player connects before load
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: Two-phase initialization (connect → load → hooks resume) creates race: if server sends entity spawns before OnGameLoaded fires, HandleEntitySpawn logs warning at 577 and returns early. Spawn queue orphaned until game loaded

brokenForSync.symptom: No error handling if PacketHandler's SEH-wrapped game thread commands (combat hits, deaths, position writes) fail — failures logged but state not rolled back
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: Commands pushed to m_commandQueue with SEH __try/__except, but if write fails (e.g., bad pointer), registry still updated and character left in inconsistent state

brokenForSync.symptom: Position updates and snapshots from packets received on network thread (PacketHandler context) — no per-entity locking vs game thread reading from interpolation
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: HandlePositionUpdate (line 783) calls interp.AddSnapshot() without locking; game thread calls interp.GetInterpolated() — classic reader/writer race. Works by luck if snapshots don't corrupt per-limb pointers

filesRead: C:/Program Files (x86)/Steam/steamapps/common/Kenshi/KenshiMP/KenshiMP.Core/net/packet_handler.cpp (full: 1795 lines, read offset 0-1254 then 1254-1795)
rawFindings.reorgNote: The net layer is architecturally sound (ENet integration clean, callback pattern clear, message dispatch systematic). Three critical issues block multiplayer reliability: (1) **Timeout cliff** — session timeout only raised after connect succeeds; during load/idle, 5s timeout causes silent disconnect. Fix: set session timeout in ConnectAsync before returning, or use adaptive timeout that ramps up after first position update. (2) **Early spawn loss** — S2C_EntitySpawn during game load are deferred with TODO (line 579) but never reprocessed. Fix: queue deferred packets in a buffer, replay after OnGameLoaded. (3) **Race on position updates** — packets arrive on network thread, interp updates happen locklessly while game thread reads them. Fix: add per-entity spinlock around snapshot add/get or use atomic compare-and-swap for individual fields. Secondary: Use KMP_KEEPALIVE_INTERVAL constant (not hardcoded 5s). Missing S2C_ZoneData/ServerInfo handlers are not in the critical path (zone system unused, ServerInfo is query-only) so lower priority.

rawFindings.subsystem: sync-registry-interp (entity lifecycle, net ID mapping, interpolation, ownership)
rawFindings.whatItActuallyDoes: This subsystem implements the core entity tracking loop: (1) EntityRegistry maintains bidirectional maps (net ID ↔ game pointer) with thread-safe read/write access for local and remote entities, distinguishing ownership (server=0, player>0). (2) EntityResolver wraps Registry with interest-set caching (3x3 zone grid), dirty-flag tracking, and compound queries. (3) Interpolation maintains per-entity ring buffers (8 snapshots) with jitter estimation, snap correction, and position/rotation lerp for rendering. (4) OwnershipManager singleton delegates all queries back to Registry (pure facade, zero actual logic). (5) The update loop: network S2C_PositionUpdate packets call AddSnapshot() (raw timestamp + pos/rot), then BackgroundInterpolate() reads remote entities and calls GetInterpolated() at render time. Snapshots are enqueued from packet_handler.cpp:~line 807, consumed by sync_orchestrator.cpp line 932-954. (6) Entity lifecycle: Register(local) → Register/RegisterRemote(netId) → SetGameObject() (transitions Spawning→Active) → Unregister on despawn.
rawFindings.wiringStatus: FULLY WIRED, linear happy path. EntityRegistry is instantiated in Core (core.h:75) and passed to: SyncOrchestrator (ctor), Interpolation (indirect via calls), EntityResolver (ctor), packet_handler (implicit via Core::GetEntityRegistry()). AddSnapshot is called from packet_handler.cpp:807 in HandlePositionUpdate. GetInterpolated is called from SyncOrchestrator::BackgroundInterpolate (line 944). SetGameObject is called from packet_handler.cpp in HandleEntitySpawn (indirect via SpawnManager), and from sync_orchestrator.cpp:774 (direct spawn fallback). SyncFacilitator (sync_facilitator.h) is a thin facade that holds references to all pieces but just delegates. OwnershipManager singleton is instantiated but only called from a few places and immediately delegates back to Registry—pure cruft.

brokenForSync.symptom: RegisterRemote creates entity in Spawning state but SetGameObject only called after NPC hijack or direct spawn succeeds. If hijack/direct spawn fails, entity remains in Spawning state indefinitely with no game object, blocking interpolation (GetInterpolated returns false). No cleanup/timeout.
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: No failure handling for async spawn attempts. NPC hijack relies on hook firing (unreliable per audit notes). Direct spawn only attempted after 15s wait + 5 attempts. If all fail, entity is orphaned.

brokenForSync.symptom: BackgroundInterpolate iterates GetRemoteEntities() at line 935, but GetRemoteEntities filters isRemote=true AND gameObject!=nullptr check is deferred to interpolation GetInterpolated. If entity is Spawning with no gameObject, interpolation still reserves space in ring buffer, wasting memory and CPU on entities that can never be rendered.
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: GetInterpolated checks (entityId in m_entities) but does not validate gameObject exists before adding snapshots. No guard in AddSnapshot.

brokenForSync.symptom: EntityRegistry::IsValidGeneration (line 374) checks generation field which is declared (entity_registry.h:13) but NEVER set anywhere. All new entities have generation=0 forever. Any check against generation will silently pass/fail unpredictably.
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: Field added in spec comment (§2.2 ghost control prevention) but never implemented in Register or RegisterRemote.

duplicatesOrOverlap: OwnershipManager (ownership.cpp:17-58) is pure facade—delegates all queries back to EntityRegistry. Can be deleted; callers should use Registry directly. SyncFacilitator (sync_facilitator.h/cpp) holds references to Registry, Interpolation, etc. and delegates all operations to them. Useful as a single-point-of-call facade for hooks/packet handlers, but not load-bearing—every operation can be done by calling the subsystems directly.
rawFindings.reorgNote: REFACTOR priorities: (1) Remove OwnershipManager singleton—it's pure boilerplate. Have packet handlers and combat hooks call Registry directly. (2) Add spawn timeout/cleanup: if RegisterRemote entity remains in Spawning state > 60s with no gameObject, auto-Unregister and log failure. (3) Implement generation field: increment on RemapEntityId, set in Register/RegisterRemote, validate in critical paths (combat, equipment writes). (4) BackgroundInterpolate should pre-filter by (gameObject != nullptr) to avoid wasting CPU on orphaned spawns. (5) Consider flattening SyncFacilitator into core.h as inline wrappers—it's purely a naming convenience and doesn't reduce coupling. (6) Document the sync loop contract clearly: packets → AddSnapshot (timestamp from SessionTime()) → GetInterpolated (at current SessionTime()) → UpdatePosition (write back to Registry). The 8-snapshot ring buffer is good; document why 8 (covers ~400ms @ 20Hz).

rawFindings.subsystem: sync-orchestrators (SyncOrchestrator, PipelineOrchestrator, SyncFacilitator, PlayerEngine, ZoneEngine, zone_interest)
rawFindings.whatItActuallyDoes: SyncOrchestrator is the actively-driven main sync pipeline that ticks every game frame (Core::OnGameTick step 3). It owns three sub-engines (EntityResolver, ZoneEngine, PlayerEngine), manages double-buffered frame data with background workers, polls/sends local entity positions every 50ms, applies interpolated remote positions, and handles spawn queue with fallback direct-spawn after 15s. PipelineOrchestrator (step 11) is a separate diagnostics/monitoring system that collects 1Hz state snapshots, broadcasts pipeline events, and runs anomaly detection—wired but NOT part of the main sync path. SyncFacilitator is a thin facade that delegates to SyncOrchestrator's engines. PlayerEngine tracks remote player state machines + session info. ZoneEngine implements 3x3 zone-based interest culling. zone_interest.cpp defines an unused singleton ZoneInterestManager that duplicates ZoneEngine's logic but is never instantiated or called.
rawFindings.wiringStatus: SyncOrchestrator: actively driven every tick via SEH_SyncOrchestratorTick(). PipelineOrchestrator: actively driven every tick (step 11, post-main-pipeline). SyncFacilitator: bound at init, used by hooks/commands to query/update engines. PlayerEngine: owned by SyncOrchestrator, called during Tick() for AFK checks (every 10s). ZoneEngine: owned by SyncOrchestrator, called every tick in StageUpdateZones(). zone_interest.cpp: DEAD CODE — ZoneInterestManager is defined but never called anywhere; Get() is never invoked.

brokenForSync.symptom: Remote character equipment/appearance not updating on remote players
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: StagePollAndSendEquipment() runs every 2s and sends diffs correctly, but remote write path disabled (per comments: 'DISABLED (use-after-free)'); faction write on remote chars DISABLED
brokenForSync.location: sync_orchestrator.cpp:591-634 (poll), core.cpp:2459-2504 (faction validation only reads, never writes to fix broken faction)

brokenForSync.symptom: Zone interest overlaps: ZoneEngine duplicates zone logic that should be in EntityResolver; zone_interest.cpp singleton is unused code clone
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: zone_interest.cpp (lines 28-60) reimplements GetInterestZones() and zone tracking identical to ZoneEngine, but never wired to Core

brokenForSync.symptom: Multiple overlapping orchestrators (sync_orchestrator, pipeline_orchestrator, old PatternOrchestrator for pattern scanning) without clear ownership
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: SyncOrchestrator is the live main sync path; PipelineOrchestrator is diagnostics-only; PatternOrchestrator in Scanner is for initial pattern discovery (separate subsystem)
brokenForSync.location: core.h:336 (SyncOrchestrator), core.h:340 (PipelineOrchestrator), kmp/orchestrator.h:153 (PatternOrchestrator for pattern scanning)

rawFindings.reorgNote: RECONCILIATION NEEDED: (1) Delete zone_interest.cpp entirely — ZoneInterestManager singleton is dead code that reimplements ZoneEngine logic. (2) Rename SyncOrchestrator to SyncPipeline for clarity (it's not orchestrating other orchestrators, it IS the pipeline). (3) Clarify that PipelineOrchestrator is a DIAGNOSTICS layer, not part of the sync path — rename to PipelineMonitor or move to sys/diagnostics. (4) Confirm equipment/appearance broken-for-sync status: StagePollAndSendEquipment() polls and sends correctly (line 591-634), but remote character writes are disabled due to use-after-free on faction. Fix requires either: (a) use proper faction assignment (not local player's), or (b) defer equipment writes until character is fully spawned + safe. (5) Zone rebuild every 500ms (StageUpdateZones:428) is correct; no overlap here.

rawFindings.subsystem: Remote Character Spawn Pipeline (spawn_manager, entity_hooks, squad_spawn_hooks, asset_facilitator)
rawFindings.whatItActuallyDoes: The spawn pipeline orchestrates remote player character creation through three mechanisms: (1) In-place NPC hijack: when the game creates an NPC via Hook_CharacterCreate, if a spawn request is pending, the hook takes over the freshly-created game object, registers it, teleports it to the remote player's position, and suppresses AI (entity_hooks.cpp:918-954). (2) Mod template factory spawn: if mod templates exist (loaded from kenshi-online.mod during post-load heap scan), SpawnWithModTemplate calls RootObjectFactory::create with the template's GameData*, creating a fully-initialized character (spawn_manager.cpp:776-820). (3) Fallback direct spawn: after 15+ seconds if in-place hijack hasn't fired, SyncOrchestrator calls SpawnCharacterDirect which uses a cloned pre-call struct and FactoryCreate (spawn_manager.cpp:130-160). Squad spawn bypass hooks into the squad spawning check to flip flags, forcing NPC creation for hijack (squad_spawn_hooks.cpp:70-136). AssetFacilitator is a thin facade delegating to LoadingOrchestrator for spawn safety checks (asset_facilitator.cpp, mostly pass-through).
rawFindings.wiringStatus: HALF-WIRED / PARTIALLY BROKEN. Entry point: packet_handler.cpp:505-725 (HandleEntitySpawn) queues spawns via SpawnManager::QueueSpawn. In-place hijack path is WIRED: entity_hooks.cpp:918-954 pops spawn requests and calls SEH_HijackNPC. Mod template path is WIRED: spawn_manager.cpp:276-284 queues, 303-474 (ProcessSpawnQueueFromHook) attempts mod templates first then falls back. Squad spawn bypass is INSTALLED but unclear if actually helping: squad_spawn_hooks.cpp:70-136 flips checks, but spawn_manager comments (line 287-301) say spawn queue is now "EXCLUSIVELY" handled by in-place replay, not squad bypass. Fallback direct spawn is WIRED in sync_orchestrator but requires pre-call data from a recent game NPC creation (15s+ wait). AssetFacilitator is BOUND in core.cpp but only referenced by sync_orchestrator and entity_hooks for CanSpawn() checks — mostly dead (asset_facilitator.cpp is 62 lines, no substantial logic). Actual live paths: (A) NPC hijack in-place replay (entity_hooks.cpp:927-943), (B) mod template from POST-LOAD heap scan (spawn_manager.cpp:698-774 FindModTemplates, called from Core::OnGameLoaded line 1557), (C) fallback to FactoryCreate if hijack queue is empty. DEAD CODE: ProcessSpawnQueue (spawn_manager.cpp:286-301) is explicitly marked "DO NOT USE" and intentionally does nothing. ProcessSpawnQueueFromHook exists but entry point unclear — appears to be designed for hook context but never called from Hook_CharacterCreate.

brokenForSync.symptom: Remote player spawn is unreliable and fragile: depends on game-generated NPC creation near the remote player's location, which may not happen if they are isolated or in a safe zone. If no NPC is created within a few seconds, spawn queue sits in SpawnManager indefinitely until SyncOrchestrator's 15+ second fallback timer fires.
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: In-place NPC hijack (the primary path) only works when Hook_CharacterCreate fires, which requires the game to naturally spawn NPCs. If the zone has no spawning logic active (e.g., building interiors, safe houses), no NPC created = no hijack opportunity. Squad spawn bypass attempts to force NPC creation via flag flipping but is only one trigger; documented to be unreliable ('fragile NPC hijack' + 'squad spawn bypass' in notes).
brokenForSync.location: entity_hooks.cpp:918-954 (hijack only works if character exists), squad_spawn_hooks.cpp:70-136 (bypass logic), spawn_manager.cpp:303-474 (ProcessSpawnQueueFromHook never called from main path)

brokenForSync.symptom: Mod template spawn path requires kenshi-online.mod to be loaded and working. If the mod is not installed or corrupted, FindModTemplates returns 0 templates and FactoryCreate path fails. Fallback to legacy template search (characterTemplates map) but those may be non-character GameData (buildings, items) causing factory crashes.
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: FindModTemplates (spawn_manager.cpp:698-774) scans heap for 'Player 1'-'Player 16' entries, picks highest numId per player assuming character has highest ID. If mod not loaded or FCS database is corrupted, candidates list is empty. OnGameLoaded (core.cpp:1405-1771) calls ScanGameDataHeap + FindModTemplates but if mod not present, m_modTemplateCount stays 0 and mod spawn path disabled. Fallback to characterTemplates from heap scan but no guarantee they are characters (could be factions, buildings with zero faction ptr).
brokenForSync.location: spawn_manager.cpp:698-774 (FindModTemplates), core.cpp:1555-1566 (called post-load), spawn_manager.cpp:347-363 (mod template spawn attempt)

brokenForSync.symptom: Position offset detection relies on fuzzy matching of float triplets in pre-call struct. If position is (0,0,0) or very small, detection may fail, leaving s_positionOffsetInStruct = -1. Remote positions won't be written correctly.
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: entity_hooks.cpp:873-890 detects position offset by reading character's spawned position and scanning pre-call struct for matching float triplets within 2.0 unit tolerance. If position is 0 or near origin, scan may match wrong offset (e.g., uninitialized data). Also, fuzzy matching may collide with other float data in struct. Only 10 attempts (s_positionDetectAttempts < 10) then gives up.

brokenForSync.symptom: GameData offset detection uses same approach but searches 8-byte aligned offsets. Rarely used (mod template path does not need it) but if spawning via legacy template, this offset is needed to swap GameData pointers in request struct. Fallback template path may not work if offset not detected.
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: entity_hooks.cpp:895-916 detects by reading character's backpointer and scanning pre-call struct. Only 10 attempts. If detection fails, m_gameDataOffsetInStruct = -1 and spawn_manager cannot customize template in pre-call struct clone.

brokenForSync.symptom: Faction write removed ('DISABLED') due to use-after-free on faction+0x250. Mod template factions may not exist in local save, but code tries not to write them (except via local player's faction in ApplyFactionFix). Remote spawned characters may have wrong faction appearance/squad membership.
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: ModTemplate approach (spawn_manager.cpp:776-820, line 807-811) explicitly skips faction write ('DO NOT call ApplyFactionFix here'). Non-mod template path writes local player's faction (spawn_manager.cpp:449, ApplyFactionFix) which causes remote chars to appear in squad panel. Faction write is fundamentally broken — mod factions unsafe, local player faction breaks squad panel UI. No solution implemented.

brokenForSync.symptom: Equipment and appearance write incomplete. RemoteCharacter spawned via hijack has whatever model/gear the NPC happened to have. No code path syncs appearance from remote player to local character.
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: entity_hooks.cpp:354-373 (SEH_HijackNPC) only teleports, renames, and disables AI. No equipment/appearance sync. packet_handler.cpp:726-815 (HandleEntitySpawn mod char link) also does not write appearance. Only player_controller.cpp handles appearance but only for local player (OnRemoteCharacterSpawned does not write equipment). Known broken per KNOWN STATE: 'equipment/appearance write to remote chars incomplete'.

rawFindings.reorgNote: The spawn pipeline has THREE competing mechanisms layered on top of each other: (1) In-place NPC hijack (primary, entity_hooks.cpp:918-954) — pops spawn queue, calls SEH_HijackNPC on freshly created NPC. (2) Mod template factory spawn (ProcessSpawnQueueFromHook, spawn_manager.cpp:303-474) — attempts mod templates via FactoryCreate, falls back to generic character template. (3) Direct spawn fallback (sync_orchestrator, 15s timer) — calls SpawnCharacterDirect using cloned pre-call struct. Squad spawn bypass (squad_spawn_hooks.cpp:70-136) flips activePlatoon flags to force NPC creation, but spawn_manager comments (lines 287-301) mark ProcessSpawnQueue as DEPRECATED and note queue is handled EXCLUSIVELY by in-place replay. Architecture is fragile because it depends on NPC creation events that may not fire in safe zones or building interiors. Code quality is HIGH (extensive SEH safety, faction validation, detailed diagnostics via VerifyReadiness) but fragmented across four modules. RECOMMENDATION: (1) Delete asset_facilitator.cpp (62-line dead facade). (2) Merge squad_spawn_hooks into sync_orchestrator (optional NPC bypass). (3) Consolidate spawn_manager and entity_hooks into single spawn orchestrator module. (4) Fix broken paths: use FactoryCreate exclusively (no NPC hijack), use mod template factions directly (skip ApplyFactionFix), implement appearance/equipment sync via protocol extension. (5) Clear spawn queue AND reset per-player counters on disconnect in one place. Current spawn_manager::VerifyReadiness (lines 822-875) provides excellent diagnostics — keep and expand. ProcessSpawnQueueFromHook and ProcessSpawnQueue are dead/deprecated code — delete. Keep SEH patterns, faction validation, and pre-call data capture logic — these are what prevent crashes.

rawFindings.whatItActuallyDoes: The system implements a polling-based position sync loop: (1) **Local position read**: `PollLocalPositions()` (core.cpp:2956) reads local player's entity positions every ~50ms via `CharacterAccessor::GetPosition()`, computes movement speed from position delta, and sends `C2S_PositionUpdate` unreliable packets. (2) **Network transport**: packets arrive and are parsed in `HandlePositionUpdate()` (packet_handler.cpp:783), which feeds snapshots into the `Interpolation` system (8-snapshot ring buffer with jitter-adaptive delay and snap correction). (3) **Remote position write**: `ApplyRemotePositionsDirect()` (core.cpp:2879) runs each tick, queries interpolated position from `Interpolation::GetInterpolated()`, and writes via `SEH_WritePositionRotation()` (core.cpp:2572) using three fallback methods: (a) direct `CharacterAccessor::setPosition()` function call (best), (b) physics chain memory write (animClass→charMovement→writable position), (c) cached position field fallback (drifts due to physics engine). Movement hooks are intentionally NOT hooked due to `mov rax,rsp` trampoline corruption; instead, position is polled and remotely-controlled characters have `MoveTo` calls suppressed via `IsRemoteControlled()` flag.
rawFindings.wiringStatus: FULLY WIRED and ACTIVE in both code paths. `OnGameTick()` (core.cpp:2097) drives the main loop: sync orchestrator path (step 2-9) calls interpolation+position write; legacy path (step 4-6) calls `PollLocalPositions()` then `ApplyRemotePositionsDirect()` explicitly. All three position-write methods are implemented and fallback correctly. Network path is complete: `C2S_PositionUpdate` sent from hook (movement_hooks.cpp:118) and poll (core.cpp:3016), `S2C_PositionUpdate` received and feeds interpolation (packet_handler.cpp:807).

brokenForSync.symptom: Position hooks declared but not installed: `movement_hooks::Install()` explicitly does NOT hook `CharacterSetPosition` or `CharacterMoveTo`
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: `mov rax, rsp` instruction at function prologue corrupts MinHook trampoline (corrupts stack frame); `CharacterMoveTo` also has undeclared 5th parameter that crashes when forwarded

brokenForSync.symptom: Hook_SetPosition and Hook_MoveTo defined but never installed; they become dead code after `Install()` returns
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: The hook installation was intentionally abandoned due to the trampoline issue; code was left as fallback/documentation but is unreachable

brokenForSync.symptom: `CharacterAccessor::WritePosition()` Method 3 (fallback) writes to cached read-only position field that physics engine overwrites each frame, causing drift on remote characters
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: Method 1 (setPosition fn) not always available; Method 2 (physics chain) requires offset discovery; fallback is unreliable but used when others fail

brokenForSync.symptom: Remote character position updates may be missed if `GetGameObject()` returns null in `ApplyRemotePositionsDirect()` — no fallback spawn, positions silently dropped
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: Remote characters must be spawned before network positions arrive; `HandleSpawnQueue()` runs after position write step, so new remote entities in frame N have no game object until frame N+1

rawFindings.reorgNote: **LOCAL POSITION READ** (WORKING): `PollLocalPositions()` correctly polls each tick at ~50ms throttle, reads via `CharacterAccessor::GetPosition()` (respects offset chain), computes speed from delta, sends `C2S_PositionUpdate` unreliable. Throttling prevents spam; change-threshold avoids network churn. **POSITION WRITE (3 METHODS, CASCADING FALLBACK)**: (1) **Method 1** – `setPosition()` function call (best, ~53ms/call on local chars) is available when pattern scan succeeds but NOT always present. (2) **Method 2** – physics chain follow (animClass+0x???→charMovement+0x???→writable position) requires offset discovery; reliable once offsets are known. (3) **Method 3** (fallback) – cached position field write drifts immediately due to physics engine overwrite; should only be used for remote entities receiving continuous updates from interpolation. **REMOTE POSITION WRITE** (STABLE): `ApplyRemotePositionsDirect()` calls `Interpolation::GetInterpolated()` to fetch smoothed position from 8-snapshot buffer, then calls `SEH_WritePositionRotation()` to write via Method 1/2/3. Wrapped in SEH to catch freed objects; marks bad game objects as null to stop crashing. **INTERPOLATION** (8-snapshot ring buffer): `AddSnapshot()` validates NaN/Inf, computes velocity from delta, detects snaps (>threshold triggers blending), stores in ring. `GetInterpolated()` uses jitter-adaptive delay (20-200ms), brackets interpolation time with bisect, linear-lerps position, slerps quaternion, applies snap correction blending. **ISSUES**: (a) Hooks not installed due to trampoline corruption; position polling is correct fallback but means missed optimization opportunities. (b) Method 3 fallback is unreliable for read-only position; remote chars should exclusively use Methods 1/2 if possible. (c) Spawn latency: remote entities' positions arrive before game objects exist; frame N has position→no-obj, frame N+1 has game object. Consider queuing snapshots until spawn completes. (d) No detection of local player's own position change via third-party code (setPosition called directly outside polling)—could cause sync lag. **REFACTOR PATH**: Consolidate three write methods into a single `WritePositionPhased()` that tries 1→2→3 cleanly; add snapshot pre-queuing for entities arriving before spawn; add optional late-binding position sync for missed frames; consider removing Method 3 unless remote char is confirmed continuously updating (vs. spawned-but-idle).

rawFindings.subsystem: Combat Sync (C2S attack intent → server resolution → S2C broadcast)
rawFindings.whatItActuallyDoes: The combat sync system relies on server-authoritative resolution rather than client-side damage application. Client-side: CharacterDeath and CharacterKO hooks capture events into a lock-free ring buffer, then ProcessDeferredEvents() (called from OnGameTick) sends C2S_CombatDeath/C2S_CombatKO messages with health snapshots via C2S_LimbHealth. Server-side: HandleCombatDeath accepts and validates reports from either victim or killer owner, broadcasts S2C_CombatDeath, and marks entity as dead. HandleCombatKO validates and broadcasts S2C_CombatKO with chest health. However, attack intent (C2S_AttackIntent) is defined in protocol.h and has HandleAttackIntent on the server but is NEVER sent from the client — ApplyDamage hook is deliberately disabled due to crash risk (mov rax,rsp prologue corruption). Thus cross-player combat (A hits B) flows only through death/KO hooks, not damage/hit events, making intermediate damage states unreliable without constant health polling.
rawFindings.wiringStatus: Half-wired. C2S_AttackIntent protocol path is dead on client side (not sent from hooks). C2S_CombatDeath/KO are sent from combat_hooks.cpp:ProcessDeferredEvents() and wired to server HandleCombatDeath/KO. Server broadcasts S2C_CombatDeath/KO. Client receives via packet_handler.cpp:HandleCombatDeath/KO which queues game thread execution via CommandQueue and applies via native CharacterDeath function with echo suppression. C2S_LimbHealth is sent as piggyback after death/KO for health state snapshot. C2S_AttackIntent caller: none found (dead code on client); server handler exists at server.cpp:844 HandleAttackIntent with full server-side combat resolution via ResolveCombat() that broadcasts S2C_CombatHit, but no client ever sends C2S_AttackIntent.

brokenForSync.symptom: Client never sends C2S_AttackIntent; cross-player damage application relies only on death/KO hooks
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: ApplyDamage hook disabled due to mov rax,rsp crash risk (combat_hooks.cpp:270-278). No alternative attack intent hook implemented. Damage visibility is purely event-driven (death/KO only), not damage-driven

brokenForSync.symptom: Health polling incomplete; intermediate limb damage not synced. Only death/KO health snapshots sent
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: C2S_LimbHealth sent only after death/KO (piggyback in ProcessDeferredEvents), not continuously. No periodic health polling thread. Limb damage between knockouts invisible to other players

brokenForSync.symptom: Echo suppression flags (s_serverSourcedDeath/KO) prevent infinite loops but create sync gaps on cross-player kills
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: When player A's character kills player B's character, only A's client detects the death hook and sends C2S_CombatDeath. B's death hook is suppressed by the S2C_CombatDeath echo. If A's death message arrives out-of-order or is dropped, B won't know they're dead until next KO event. No cross-reporting fallback

brokenForSync.symptom: Combat hit visual feedback missing. S2C_CombatHit broadcast by server but never applied to client
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: Server.cpp:844-885 HandleAttackIntent builds MsgCombatHit and broadcasts S2C_CombatHit, but packet_handler.cpp:850-926 HandleCombatHit only logs and applies damage via native ApplyDamage (if attacker exists) or direct health write. No visual effect, sound, or animation playback. No acknowledgment that the hit landed
brokenForSync.location: KenshiMP.Server/server.cpp:844-914 (HandleAttackIntent creates S2C_CombatHit); KenshiMP.Core/net/packet_handler.cpp:850-926 (HandleCombatHit does not apply effects)

brokenForSync.symptom: ResolveCombat server-side resolution unused. Client controls combat outcome via death hook order
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: ResolveCombat at combat_resolver.cpp:59-119 computes damage, KO/death, and body part selection on server. HandleAttackIntent at server.cpp:865 calls it but no C2S_AttackIntent ever arrives. Thus server resolution never executes. Client death hooks are authoritative instead
brokenForSync.location: KenshiMP.Server/combat_resolver.cpp:59-119 (ResolveCombat never called); KenshiMP.Server/server.cpp:844-914 (HandleAttackIntent unreachable from client)

duplicatesOrOverlap: combat_resolver.cpp is dead code — server can compute combat but never receives C2S_AttackIntent from client to trigger it
HandleAttackIntent server handler is dead — wired to protocol but client never sends the message
ApplyDamage hook is deliberately disabled (not a mistake, a known crash vector per comments)
rawFindings.reorgNote: Combat sync is in a broken half-state. The architecture decision to NOT hook ApplyDamage (due to crash risk) left a gap: without damage events, only death/KO hooks fire, so intermediate damage states are invisible. The server-side combat resolver exists but is unreachable because C2S_AttackIntent is never sent. To fix: (1) Either hook attacks at a safer point (not ApplyDamage) and send C2S_AttackIntent+client-side prediction with server validation, OR (2) Use continuous health polling on a timer for remote characters and disable local damage application. Current system is worse than either: it's event-driven but only for end states (death/KO), creating ambiguity on who killed whom in multi-player melee. The echo suppression flags work but don't solve the fundamental issue that intermediate damage is invisible. combat_resolver.cpp should be deleted if C2S_AttackIntent remains unsent, or the entire client damage-hook layer should be redesigned if we want server-authoritative combat.

rawFindings.subsystem: inventory-equipment sync (item pickup/drop/equipment slots for remote character appearance)
rawFindings.whatItActuallyDoes: The inventory/equipment subsystem hooks three game functions (ItemPickup, ItemDrop, BuyItem) to detect when local players add/remove/trade items. These hooks send C2S_ItemPickup/C2S_ItemDrop/C2S_TradeRequest to the server, which broadcasts S2C_InventoryUpdate to all other clients. The sync orchestrator polls local character equipment every 2 seconds and sends C2S_EquipmentUpdate for any changes. The server broadcasts these equipment changes as S2C_EquipmentUpdate messages. Client packet handler applies incoming equipment updates and inventory updates by writing to the item stack counts or calling SetEquipment(). Resource hooks exist as a stub for future Ogre3D resource manager hooking (not yet implemented).
rawFindings.wiringStatus: HALF-WIRED. Hooks installed and firing (inventory_hooks.cpp:199-220). Local→server works. Server→remote client wired (packet_handler.cpp:1424-1443, 1474-1498). But equipment NOT synced on spawn (no equipment in S2C_EntitySpawn, only on zone request at server.cpp:1366-1384). SetEquipment() incomplete (game_inventory.cpp:129-179): requires items in inventory first, fails on remote chars. No proactive remote equipment sync.

brokenForSync.symptom: Remote players spawn without equipped items visible
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: S2C_EntitySpawn does not include initial equipment array. Equipment sent separately only via HandleZoneRequest (server.cpp:1366-1384), never during HandleEntitySpawn. New spawns never receive their equipped state.

brokenForSync.symptom: SetEquipment() fails if item not already in inventory
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: InventoryAccessor::SetEquipment scans inventory list, finds matching template ID, writes pointer to equipment slot. If no match, returns false silently. Cannot equip items that haven't been picked up.

brokenForSync.symptom: Equipment changes via UI (drag, swap, unequip) never sync to other players
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: inventory_hooks only intercepts ItemPickup/ItemDrop/BuyItem game functions. UI-only equipment operations bypass these hooks entirely. Sync orchestrator only polls LOCAL player equipment (sync_orchestrator.cpp:600).

brokenForSync.symptom: Resource hooks unimplemented, gracefully degrade
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: TryDiscoverOgreManagers() placeholder returns false always (resource_hooks.cpp:49-77). TODO comment: need runtime Ogre VTable index discovery.

rawFindings.whatItActuallyDoes: Faction sync captures relation changes via Hook_FactionRelation, broadcasts them through the server (HandleFactionRelation), and applies them to remote characters by resolving faction pointers via CharacterIterator. Building sync has 5 hooks (Place, Destroyed, Dismantle, Construct, Repair) that are DEFINED but NEVER INSTALLED; the code exists but remains unreachable. Faction writes to remote characters are intentionally DISABLED due to use-after-free: writing an NPC faction pointer causes crashes when that faction's zone unloads (dereferenced at game+0x927E94). Faction strings are patched in .rdata at known Steam/GOG offsets to match the server-assigned mod faction, allowing player factions to persist across zones without dangling pointers.
rawFindings.wiringStatus: Faction relation hook IS called live: Hook_FactionRelation fires when local player changes faction relations, sends C2S_FactionRelation, server broadcasts S2C_FactionRelation to all players, packet_handler::HandleFactionRelation applies it. Faction write to remotes is intentionally DEAD: code exists (WriteFaction in game_character.cpp:385) but never called on remote characters after spawn — OnRemoteCharacterSpawned skips it (player_controller.cpp:159-167). Building hooks are completely DEAD CODE: building_hooks.cpp defines all 5 hook functions (Place, Destroyed, Dismantle, Construct, Repair) but building_hooks::Install() is never called from anywhere. No caller found in core.cpp hook initialization sequence — building_hooks included as header but never invoked.

brokenForSync.symptom: Faction write disabled on remote characters — they retain factory-assigned faction instead of capturing player's faction
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: use-after-free crash: writing NPC faction pointers fails when source zone unloads (faction object freed but still referenced)

brokenForSync.symptom: Building sync hooks defined but never installed — building placement/destruction/repair not synced to other players
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: building_hooks::Install() unreachable from Core initialization; hook infrastructure exists but integration missing

brokenForSync.symptom: BuildingConstruct and BuildingRepair hooks log but do not send packets — construction progress and repair amounts not synced
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: Incomplete implementation: hooks defined but packet sending code omitted (only logging present)

rawFindings.reorgNote: FACTION: Keep Hook_FactionRelation (faction_hooks.cpp:36-70) and packet path — it works. Keep faction string patching (lobby_manager.cpp) — elegant solution to avoid use-after-free. DOCUMENT the intentional skip of remote faction writes (player_controller.cpp:159-167): use-after-free is real (zone-unload → freed faction object). For MVP, remotes retain factory faction (mod templates have guaranteed persistent factions). If MVP needs player factions on remotes, must use SERVER-ASSIGNED player faction (not captured NPC pointers), but this requires protocol changes (server tracks player faction per entity). BUILDING: Delete dead hooks code from building_hooks.cpp (entire file) OR stub it out and defer to post-MVP. The hook infrastructure works (see entity_hooks, combat_hooks working fine) but building data model is incomplete: (1) buildings not materialized on remote clients (S2C_BuildPlaced only registers ghost entity, no game object call), (2) no building ownership/permission sync, (3) construct/repair progress incomplete. Building sync is safely deferrable for MVP — buildings sync as position entities only. Complete rewrite post-MVP: define ServerEntity building payload, add BuildingAccessor read/write, populate GameDataTemplate for remote buildings, then revisit hooks. Current code pollutes the codebase with 350 lines of unreachable plumbing."

rawFindings.whatItActuallyDoes: The system manages initial player connection, faction assignment, and multiplayer save loading. LobbyManager patches a faction string into game memory based on server assignment. Loading_orchestrator tracks game load phases via burst detection and gates spawn timing. Shared_save_sync discovers pre-existing "Player N" characters in the shared save by name and syncs their positions via unreliable position updates. Save_hooks, world_hooks, and time_hooks provide supporting infrastructure but are partially disabled or incomplete.
rawFindings.wiringStatus: PARTIALLY WIRED: (1) LobbyManager.OnFactionAssigned() called from S2C_FactionAssignment handler (packet_handler.cpp:1758), ApplyFactionPatch() called at game-ready phase. (2) LoadingOrchestrator wired into OnGameTick (core.cpp lines 2326, 2409), burst detection calls OnBurstDetected/Ended. (3) shared_save_sync::Update() called every tick (core.cpp:2357, 2439), lazily calls Init() when faction arrives. (4) C2S_PlayerReady sent from OnGameLoaded (core.cpp:1653), server responds with S2C_AllPlayersReady (server.cpp:2425). MISSING WIRING: C2S_LobbyReady is defined and handled (server.cpp:2089) but NEVER SENT by client — only available via manual /ready command. No coordination layer ensures both players have loaded the save before spawning mod characters.

brokenForSync.symptom: C2S_LobbyReady message protocol is defined but never auto-sent by client
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: Lobby-phase handshake (faction assignment → ready confirmation → start game) was planned but incomplete. Only game-ready phase (C2S_PlayerReady/S2C_AllPlayersReady) is wired. C2S_LobbyReady exists only in /ready builtin command.

brokenForSync.symptom: No guarantee both players have loaded their save game before character spawning begins
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: S2C_AllPlayersReady fires when all players send C2S_PlayerReady (after game load), but if player 2's save-load is slow, player 1 may spawn characters before player 2's 'Player N' mod characters are loaded from disk.
brokenForSync.location: KenshiMP.Core/core.cpp:1648-1657 (C2S_PlayerReady sent without load coordination), KenshiMP.Server/server.cpp:2405-2436 (HandlePlayerReady does not track actual save load completion)

brokenForSync.symptom: shared_save_sync::Init() is never explicitly called; relies on lazy init inside Update()
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: Init() is deferred until Update() detects faction assignment (shared_save_sync.cpp:188-193). This works but is fragile: if Update() is called before faction arrives, it will retry every tick. No explicit initialization hook.

brokenForSync.symptom: save_hooks are disabled; Save/Load functions never instrumented
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: Function signatures unverified; calling through bad trampolines caused crashes during save load. Disabled to pass-through (save_hooks.cpp:42-49).
brokenForSync.location: KenshiMP.Core/hooks/save_hooks.cpp:42-50 (Install returns true but does nothing)

brokenForSync.symptom: world_hooks BuildingPlace hook DISABLED; no building sync on zone load
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: Signature unverified, zone-load crashes (world_hooks.cpp:153-154)

duplicatesOrOverlap: sync_orchestrator (KenshiMP.Core/sync/sync_orchestrator.*) also tracks pipeline events and phase transitions; LoadingOrchestrator duplicates burst-to-phase logic
player_engine (if exists) may overlap with LobbyManager faction tracking
Multiple overlapping 'ready' state: LobbyManager.HasFaction(), LoadingOrchestrator.IsGameLoaded(), shared_save_sync.s_initialized, Core.m_gameLoaded
rawFindings.reorgNote:
MISSING PIECE: A clean lobby-coordination layer. Current design splits across three separate systems:
1. **Faction Assignment** (LobbyManager): patches memory on S2C_FactionAssignment. No acknowledgment sent.
2. **Game Load Handshake** (LoadingOrchestrator + Core::OnGameLoaded): fires C2S_PlayerReady when game loads.
3. **Character Discovery** (shared_save_sync): lazily scans for "Player N" by name after faction assigned.

PROBLEMS:
- Lobby phase (faction setup) never closes: no C2S_LobbyReady sent automatically.
- shared_save_sync is ONLY for 2-player shared-save mod, not for general multiplayer.
- No orchestrated "all players loaded" event before first character spawn.
- ApplyFactionPatch() called reactively (when S2C_FactionAssignment arrives) vs. proactively (before save load).

SOLUTION FOR CLEAN REBUILD:
1. **Lobby Phase** (0xF1/0xF2): Auto-send C2S_LobbyReady once faction received + memory patched. Server waits for all, broadcasts S2C_LobbyStart. Clients transition to "Loading" phase.
2. **Load Phase**: Game loads. LoadingOrchestrator tracks via burst + OnGameLoaded event.
3. **Ready Phase** (0x09/0x0A): After game loaded, send C2S_PlayerReady. Server broadcasts S2C_AllPlayersReady. Clients spawn.
4. **Character Discovery** (mod-agnostic): Use entity spawn protocol (C2S_EntitySpawnReq) for all remote players, not name-matching hacks.

KEEP:
- LobbyManager.OnFactionAssigned() + ApplyFactionPatch() (faction string injection is necessary).
- LoadingOrchestrator phase machine and spawn gating (robust burst detection + settle timers).
- shared_save_sync.Update() for 2-player mod sync (low-level position polling).
- time_hooks: TimeUpdate hook + SetServerTime (game speed synced from server).

REFACTOR:
- Remove lazy Init() from shared_save_sync; call Init() explicitly when S2C_FactionAssignment lands.
- Replace C2S_LobbyReady manual command with auto-send after ApplyFactionPatch succeeds.
- Unify ready-state tracking: one state machine, not scattered atomics.
- Add S2C_GameLoadNotification (server tells clients when peer entered "Loading" phase).

DELETE:
- save_hooks (disabled anyway; local saves work fine without hooks).
- BuildingPlace hook (unverified signature; no building sync implemented).
- Manual /ready command (replace with auto-send or UI prompt).

rawFindings.whatItActuallyDoes: The hooking subsystem provides pattern scanning, function resolution, MinHook integration, and the MovRaxRsp fix for x64 MSVC functions. Core components: (1) PatternScanner (scanner.cpp) — IDA-style byte pattern matching on .text section with RIP resolution, (2) RuntimeStringScanner (patterns.cpp) — fallback string-xref analysis with .pdata validation for function boundaries, (3) HookManager (hook_manager.cpp) — MinHook wrapper with per-hook MovRaxRsp naked detour + trampoline wrapper generation, (4) MovRaxRspFix (mov_rax_rsp_fix.cpp) — two runtime-generated ASM stubs to fix RBP/RSP aliasing corruption in hooked functions. Also includes: (5) PatternOrchestrator (orchestrator.cpp) — multi-phase discovery pipeline (PE analysis, strings, vtables, patterns, fallbacks, call graph), (6) ScannerEngine — SIMD/batch scanning wrapper, (7) PDataEnumerator — .pdata enumeration for function boundaries, (8) FunctionAnalyzer — prologue analysis, (9) VTableScanner, StringAnalyzer, CallGraphAnalyzer — supporting RE tools.
rawFindings.wiringStatus: HEAVILY WIRED but architecturally complex. Core wiring: (A) patterns.cpp::ResolveGameFunctions() is called at startup by core.cpp::InitializeHooks(), uses patterns + RuntimeStringScanner fallback. (B) HookManager is wired to patterns.cpp — each pattern result becomes a hook target via HookManager::Install(). (C) MovRaxRspFix is AUTOMATICALLY applied per hook in HookManager::InstallRaw() — detects "mov rax,rsp" prologue and wraps with naked detour + wrapper. (D) PatternOrchestrator is REGISTERED but appears underutilized — initialized in core.cpp but most hooks use direct ResolveGameFunctions() instead. (E) VTable discovery (RTTI) is wired to SquadAddMember resolution in orchestrator.cpp::RegisterBuiltinPatterns() — uses fuzzy "ActivePlatoon|Platoon|Squad" matching. (F) Dispatcher: core.cpp calls ResolveGameFunctions(scanner, funcs) which populates GameFunctions pointers, then HookManager installs each.

brokenForSync.symptom: MovRaxRsp reentrancy guard assumes single-threaded game logic but uses global depth counter at OFF_DEPTH offset in allocated page
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: If the game ever calls a hooked mov-rax-rsp function recursively or from multiple call sites in parallel, the depth counter corruption will cause the 2nd call to see depth>1, skip normal path, and JMP to raw trampoline — bypassing the C++ hook entirely. This breaks all multiplayer sync for recursive calls.

brokenForSync.symptom: FindFunctionStart walks back 16384 bytes (line 224) but only after .pdata fails
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: For a 100KB function, the 2048->16384 increase helps, but the 16-byte alignment rejection (patterns.cpp:386-400) was supposed to be removed — it's now only warning-level. However, the 16-byte alignment check WILL still reject some valid vtable-discovered functions if .pdata returns no match, causing hook installation to fail silently.

brokenForSync.symptom: ApplyDamage is a 6925-byte function — the 4KB scan window in patterns.cpp::FindGlobalNearString (line 68) is inadequate
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: FindGlobalNearString scans [funcStart, xref+512] which assumes globals are loaded early. For a 6925-byte function where the global is loaded at +6000, this scan misses it. The .pdata fallback (line 619-624) extends to 4KB but still may miss late loads. PlayerBase discovery could fail or return wrong address.

brokenForSync.symptom: Three validator bugs noted but code has partial mitigations: (1) FindFunctionStart 2048→16384 walk-back increased; (2) 16-byte alignment check demoted to warning for .pdata-confirmed entries; (3) ApplyDamage marked broken
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: The mitigations are incomplete — they address symptoms but not the root problem (pattern scanner's FindFunctionStart is fragile). SquadAddMember 16-byte alignment rejection persists at line 386 check even though it's now warning-only. The overlap check (offset <= 0x10) is reasonable but the reject (line 397) happens before the warning.

brokenForSync.symptom: HookManager::Enable/Disable use MH_EnableHook/MH_DisableHook for non-MovRaxRsp hooks, which re-patches function bytes + suspends ALL threads
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: This is architecturally unsafe during game loop — mid-frame Enable/Disable can corrupt the call stack if a thread is mid-call. The bypass flag (for MovRaxRsp) avoids this, but standard hooks remain unsafe. No thread-safety wrapper exists for dynamic hook enable/disable.

brokenForSync.symptom: HookManager::Shutdown only DISABLES hooks, never calls MH_RemoveHook or MH_Uninitialize (comment at line 34-37)
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: Rationale is correct (game atexit handlers may call freed trampolines), but this leaks MinHook's internal state — all hook structs remain in MinHook's hook list. On hot-reload or module re-init, MH_Initialize would fail or MH_CreateHook calls on same addresses would collide.

rawFindings.reorgNote: REFACTORING RECOMMENDATIONS: (1) **Reentrancy Safety**: The MovRaxRsp depth counter (OFF_DEPTH at line 305) must become thread-local or per-call-context to survive reentrant calls. Current code will silently drop hooks on recursion. Alternative: use per-function depth counter, not global page-wide. (2) **Validator Robustness**: Consolidate the three FindFunctionStart codepaths (.pdata + 16KB walk-back + alignment check) into a single coherent validator that reports decision rationale. Remove the 0x10 offset limit check before the warning (patterns.cpp:364-400) — demote to info-level diagnostic, not a rejection. (3) **Dynamic Hook Enable/Disable Safety**: Add a spinlock/wait around MH_EnableHook/MH_DisableHook to prevent thread collision, or migrate all dynamic hooks to the bypass-flag approach (like MovRaxRsp). For now, document that Enable/Disable are NOT safe mid-frame. (4) **PatternOrchestrator Integration**: The orchestrator is well-designed but underused. Migrate core.cpp to use RegisterBuiltinPatterns() + Run() instead of calling ResolveGameFunctions() directly. This will enable multi-method fallback chains and better diagnostics. (5) **Global Pointer Discovery**: Extend FindGlobalNearString scan window from 512 to 8KB for large functions (use .pdata for accurate function bounds). Test against ApplyDamage (6925B) to ensure globals are found. (6) **MinHook State Leakage**: Consider adding a hook deregistration API for testing/reload scenarios, but document that it's unsafe during live gameplay."

rawFindings.whatItActuallyDoes: The SDK layer consists of two main components: (1) KenshiSDK provides polling-based world snapshots via CharacterIterator + CharacterAccessor, computing per-frame diffs of entity positions/health/rotations. (2) VisualProxy manages interpolation state for remote players (Phase 1 state-only, Phase 2 Ogre rendering pending). Both are initialized at game load but SDK snapshot/diff methods are never queried — the live code bypasses SDK and directly uses CharacterAccessor within hooks and sync orchestrator instead.
rawFindings.wiringStatus: KenshiSDK is wired for initialization (core.cpp:1760) and Update() (core.cpp:2351, 2433) but none of its query methods (GetCurrentSnapshot, GetLastDiff, GetEntityState) are called anywhere in the codebase. VisualProxy is partially wired: initialized (core.cpp:1767), Update() called (core.cpp:2354, 2436), and CreateProxy called from packet_handler (net/packet_handler.cpp) for remote entities, but rendering is explicitly deferred (Phase 2, comment at visual_proxy.h:19-25). The actual sync path uses CharacterAccessor directly in hooks (movement_hooks.cpp:88, combat_hooks.cpp) and sync_orchestrator (sync_orchestrator.cpp:24, 78) for position/health reads/writes.

brokenForSync.symptom: SDK polling data never used for multiplayer sync
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: KenshiSDK.Update() populates snapshots/diffs but GetCurrentSnapshot/GetLastDiff/GetEntityState methods have zero callers. Sync instead reads via CharacterAccessor directly per-hook.

brokenForSync.symptom: VisualProxy rendering layer permanently deferred with no Ogre integration path
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: visual_proxy.h:19-25 document Phase 1 (state tracking only) vs Phase 2 (Ogre rendering), but no code path exists to transition. ProxyData.sceneNode and ogreEntity pointers are allocated (line 108-109) but never populated or written to.

brokenForSync.symptom: No unified sync entry point — mixed paths (CharacterAccessor direct + SDK polling)
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: Architecture shift to SDK abstraction (2026-03-26) is incomplete. Code still scattered: hooks use CharacterAccessor directly, core.cpp has hand-rolled position polling in PollLocalPositions (2956-3039), movement_hooks has position send throttle (35-100). SDK was built as abstraction but real sync continues ad-hoc.
brokenForSync.location: core.cpp:2956 (PollLocalPositions) vs sdk/kenshi_sdk.cpp:194-217 (SDK polling) vs hooks/movement_hooks.cpp:87-100

rawFindings.reorgNote: The SDK layer is well-designed structurally (clean offset abstraction, proper diff computation, thread-safe snapshots) but is incomplete-to-orphaned in integration. Recommendation: (1) **Unify sync path**: Route all character reads through KenshiSDK.GetCurrentSnapshot() → GetLastDiff() in core.cpp, replacing PollLocalPositions hand-rolled polling. (2) **Wire SDK diffs to network**: Make KenshiSDK the source-of-truth for what changed, feed diffs directly to packet builder rather than per-hook updates. (3) **Phase in VisualProxy rendering**: Either complete Phase 2 (discover Ogre::SceneManager, attach nodes) or delete ProxyData.sceneNode/ogreEntity fields if rendering is permanently deferred. Currently it's zombie code. (4) **Consolidate duplicates**: Remove WorldAccessor singleton from game_world.cpp (duplicate of GameWorldAccessor), standardize on one memory-access pattern. The game_types.h CharacterAccessor/SquadAccessor/etc. are correct — keep those as low-level primitives; SDK should be the high-level orchestrator."

rawFindings.subsystem: UI & Command System (Native MyGUI HUD, Menu, Chat, Slash Commands)
rawFindings.whatItActuallyDoes: KenshiMP implements a 3-layer native MyGUI UI: (1) NativeHud — in-game HUD with connection status bar, debug/loading log, chat panel (10 lines), player list (8 rows), auto-hiding after 15s game-load; (2) NativeMenu — main menu panel (F1 toggle) with JOIN/HOST/SETTINGS/SERVER BROWSER tabs, EditBox-based IP/port/name input, server browser with query client for favorites; (3) Slash commands via CommandRegistry — 20+ builtins (/tp, /sync, /players, /time, /status, /connect, etc.) that tunnel through native chat (Enter key opens NativeHud's chat input, "/" prefix routes to command handler, result becomes system message). WndProc hook (render_hooks.cpp:73–223) drives all input: F1 toggles menu, Enter toggles chat, Tab toggles playerlist, Backtick toggles debug, Escape closes UI, Alt+chars/backspace/return handled modally per active widget. MyGuiBridge (mygui_bridge.cpp) is a thin dllexport resolver to MyGUIEngine_x64.dll (layout load, widget find, caption/property set via GetProcAddress). Overlay manages connection state machine (auto-connect on game load with 2s delay, retry 6x over 30s, detect server loss, teleport remote entities underground on disconnect). TaskOrchestrator runs 2 worker threads with SEH protection for background entity reads/interpolation — frame-work commands block game thread. FrameData is double-buffered position cache (local entity reads, interpolated remote results, pre-built packet bytes).
rawFindings.wiringStatus: FULLY WIRED. WndProc hooks at render_hooks.cpp:73–223, dispatches to NativeHud/NativeMenu. NativeHud::Init() called every frame in Update() with retry backoff (1s×10, then 10s); nativeMenu.Init() called on demand when F1 pressed. CommandRegistry::RegisterBuiltins() called once in Core::Initialize(). Chat message handler in SendChatMessage (native_hud.cpp:577–613) checks "/" prefix, executes command, displays result. Overlay.Update() handles connection state, auto-connect gate, retry logic (50 frame throttle, 5s retry interval). All inputs consumed modally via WndProc to prevent double-input from OIS. No dead code detected — every component is called.

brokenForSync.symptom: Chat input capture may conflict with game OIS input if WndProc hook fires AFTER OIS has already consumed keystroke. Modal gates (chatActive/menuVisible check) happen in WndProc but rely on state flags that may not be synced if OIS event loop runs in parallel.
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: WndProc hook order relative to OIS event pump is implicit; no explicit lock ensures WndProc-set flags are visible before OIS re-fires.

brokenForSync.symptom: Server browser query results populated every 30 frames (native_menu.cpp:229–260, overlay.cpp:228–260) but query client may be slow or stalled on lossy network. No timeout to show 'TIMEOUT' or auto-fallback to manual entry.
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: ServerQueryClient lacks deadline or result expiry; slow server query never times out visually.

brokenForSync.symptom: Chat panel auto-hides after 30s with no activity (native_hud.cpp:353–361) but does NOT re-show when new message arrives while hidden. Player may miss incoming messages.
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: UpdateChatDisplay() does not re-show chat panel if new message arrives (only checks m_chatHistory.empty()). System messages arrive but panel stays hidden.

brokenForSync.symptom: NativeHud::Init() retries every 1–10s until MyGuiBridge ready, but MyGuiBridge::Init() can hang if MyGUIEngine_x64.dll loads but singletons are not yet alive (LayoutManager/Gui return null). Entire HUD remains invisible until MyGUI fully initializes, with only debug log visible.
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: Startup race: Kenshi's MyGUI initialization is asynchronous. NativeHud tries to init before MyGUI singletons exist, fails silently, logs 'Singletons not ready' every 10s.

brokenForSync.symptom: Menu 'MULTIPLAYER' button on main menu only clickable between 15–∞ seconds after startup (IsMainMenuReady gate at render_hooks.cpp:62–68). If user clicks during logo/splash (~0–15s), nothing happens. No visual feedback that button is 'not ready'.
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: Hard 15s delay to let Kenshi load resources. If Kenshi's logo is quick or slow, timing mismatch causes user confusion.

brokenForSync.symptom: Auto-connect in Overlay only fires if m_autoConnectPending=true AND game loads in Loading phase AND 2s elapses (overlay.cpp:58–93). No UI message if auto-connect fails silently (rare edge case).
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: Auto-connect only visible if user set it explicitly or via server-browser row selection. If config.autoConnect=false (hardcoded in overlay.cpp:30), auto-connect never triggers even if set.

rawFindings.reorgNote:
**ARCHITECTURE ASSESSMENT:**

The UI system is **clean and well-separated**, with clear responsibilities:

1. **MyGuiBridge** — Pure abstraction layer (no business logic), wraps MyGUI via GetProcAddress + SEH. Solid for ABI isolation. No changes needed.

2. **NativeHud** — In-game HUD (status bar, chat, player list, debug log). Well-designed modular state machine. MINOR ISSUE: chat panel should re-show on new message even if hidden.

3. **NativeMenu** — Main menu (F1 panel). Solid layout navigation, EditBox focus tracking, button hit-detection via normalized screen coords. Works well.

4. **Overlay** — Connection orchestration (auto-connect, retry, disconnect, server browser query). Layered cleanly with NativeMenu but could benefit from explicit state machine enum (currently scattered bools).

5. **CommandRegistry + Builtin Commands** — Excellent extensibility. 20+ debug/player commands well-implemented. No duplication. ✓

6. **WndProc Hook** — Modal input dispatch is correct: F1/Tab/Enter/Backtick/Escape key handlers, then modal gate (chatActive/menuVisible) consume all WM_CHAR/WM_KEYDOWN. Good design. Slight timing risk if OIS fires after hook.

7. **TaskOrchestrator** — Worker thread pool with frame-work gating. SEH protection on workers is correct. Frame-blocking strategy is solid.

**RECOMMENDED REFACTORS:**

1. **Chat panel auto-hide bug** (native_hud.cpp:363–367): Change `hasContent = !m_chatHistory.empty() || m_chatInputActive` to also show panel if system messages just arrived. Track a flag per frame.

2. **Overlay connection state** (overlay.cpp:20–180): Replace scattered bools (m_autoConnectPending, m_autoConnectDone, m_connecting, m_retryPending, m_gameLoadedTimerStarted) with explicit enum:
   ```cpp
   enum class ConnectionState { Disconnected, WaitingForGameLoad, Connecting, Connected, Reconnecting };
   ```
   Reduces bugs from state combinations.

3. **MyGuiBridge init race** (mygui_bridge.cpp:263–270): Instead of silently retrying, log when singletons are null. Consider a 'stale' flag to avoid retry spam.

4. **Server query timeout** (overlay.cpp:228–260): Add 10s timeout to query results. Show "TIMEOUT" for servers that don't respond.

5. **Main menu button readiness** (render_hooks.cpp:62–68): Instead of hard 15s delay, detect when MyGUI is actually ready (check MyGuiBridge::IsReady()). Or dim the button visually until ready.

6. **Command execution logging**: builtin_commands.cpp already logs all commands to spdlog, which is good. Consider also logging to NativeHud system messages for user visibility (e.g., "/connect 127.0.0.1:27800" should show "Connecting..." in-chat).

**QOL IMPROVEMENTS (NOT BUGS):**

- Auto-complete for command names (tab-key while typing "/p" suggests "/players", "/pos", "/ping").
- History for chat/console (up/down arrow in chat input).
- Copy/paste support in EditBoxes (Ctrl+C/V).
- Player ping display in player list or hover tooltip.
- Server browser sort by ping/player count.

**SUMMARY:** The UI stack is **production-ready** with only minor polish needed. The architecture is sound: clear layers, good input handling, no dead code, proper thread safety. Main bug is chat panel not re-showing on new messages (easy fix). Connection state could be more explicit (enum refactor, low risk). No reorganization needed; focus on the 1–2 identified bugs and optional QOL features.

rawFindings.whatItActuallyDoes: The dedicated server is a complete authority hub for multiplayer state synchronization. It operates a per-tick server loop (main.cpp:119-132, 50-200ms ticks), manages 16 player slots via ConnectedPlayer structs (server.h:17-29), maintains an authoritative entity registry in m_entities (server.h:146), broadcasts positions every tick (BroadcastPositions), syncs time every 5s (BroadcastTimeSync), and detects/cleans orphan entities (30s cleanup). It validates all client-reported actions (ownership, distance, NaN checks), resolves combat server-side, persists world state to JSON (world_persistence.cpp), auto-saves every 60s, and registers with optional master server for listing.
rawFindings.wiringStatus: WELL-WIRED. The server is the live authority: HandlePacket (server.cpp:310) dispatches 25+ message types from clients, HandlePositionUpdate validates and stores (server.cpp:789), HandleAttackIntent delegates to ResolveCombat and broadcasts results (server.cpp:844), HandleEntitySpawnReq creates server-authoritative IDs (server.cpp:1094), HandleLobbyReady checks all-ready condition (server.cpp:2088), master connection polls events and heartbeats (UpdateMasterConnection server.cpp:2344). The main loop (main.cpp:115-132) calls server.Update() every tick, which pumps ENet events, broadcasts, and flushes packets. All message handlers are called via the packet dispatch (server.cpp:340-529). Master server integration is optional but fully implemented.

brokenForSync.symptom: HandlePlayerReady has wrong parameter type and calls undefined methods
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: Copy-paste error: declared as 'ConnectedPlayer& player' in header (server.h:119) but implemented as 'Player& player' (server.cpp:2405). Calls GetReadyPlayerCount()/GetConnectedPlayerCount() (server.cpp:2438-2451) which iterate p.connected and p.isReady on m_players, but ConnectedPlayer has no .connected field (struct at server.h:17-29 uses lobbyReady and isReady).

brokenForSync.symptom: Compilation failure in HandlePlayerReady and companion methods
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: Methods reference undefined struct 'Player' and undefined methods BroadcastReliable(), and iterate m_players with fields that don't exist on ConnectedPlayer. Implementation does not match the server.h header declaration (signature mismatch).

brokenForSync.symptom: Zone-based interest culling disabled in BroadcastPositions
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: Code comment at server.cpp:1001-1003 states 'zone filtering disabled for small player counts — 16-slot server doesn't need spatial culling, and zone mismatch was preventing players from ever seeing each other'. Sends all non-owned entities to each player without zone filtering. This breaks proper zone interest model.

brokenForSync.symptom: Master server heartbeat only sent if m_masterConnected
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: At server.cpp:2269, SendMasterHeartbeat() returns early if !m_masterConnected. But m_masterConnected is only set to true on ENET_EVENT_TYPE_CONNECT (server.cpp:2352). If connection succeeds but the event is missed or races, heartbeats never send. No retry on timeout.

duplicatesOrOverlap: GameStateManager (game_state.h/cpp) — duplicates simple time/weather logic that is already in GameServer.m_timeOfDay/m_weatherState (server.h:155-156). GameStateManager is declared but never instantiated or called by the server.

EntityManager (entity_manager.h/cpp) — pure utility statics (GetEntitiesByOwner, GetEntitiesInZone, etc.) that duplicate queries GameServer performs inline (e.g., server.cpp:1005-1008 iterates entities itself). These could be called but are not used.

ZoneManager (zone_manager.h/cpp) — zone population tracking and interest helpers (GetRelevantEntities, ShouldReceiveUpdates) are implemented but BroadcastPositions (server.cpp:994) does not use them — ignores zone filtering intentionally (comment at 1001-1003).

PlayerManager (player_manager.h/cpp) — name lookup, banning, AFK detection, rate limiting utilities are defined but never called from GameServer. Server uses inline checks (e.g., peer address parsing in IsLoopbackAddress, server.cpp:15-17).
rawFindings.reorgNote: VERDICT: The server core IS the authoritative sync hub and should be kept. The per-tick loop, message dispatch, entity validation, and broadcasting are correctly wired. HOWEVER, fix the HandlePlayerReady compilation error (lines 2405-2451) immediately: correct parameter type and remove calls to non-existent methods, or refactor to use inline checks on m_players. The zone-filtering disabling (comment at 1001-1003) is intentional for a 16-player server but should be documented as a deliberate tradeoff. The master server integration is optional but complete. CLEANUP: Delete or consolidate GameStateManager (use server.m_timeOfDay directly), EntityManager (fold into inline queries or lazy-instantiate), ZoneManager (not called, but leave as optional interest-mgmt layer for future optimization), and PlayerManager (not called, keep for future ban/AFK/rate-limit features). The server fundamentally works — it validates ownership, broadcasts positions, resolves combat, persists state, and maintains player slots. Focus refactoring on fixing the compilation error and consolidating utility classes into lazy/optional imports."

rawFindings.whatItActuallyDoes: Win32 GUI launcher with 5-step installation: copy KenshiMP.Core.dll to Kenshi root, append Plugin=KenshiMP.Core to Plugins_x64.cfg, deploy kenshi-online.mod to data/ and mods/, write client.json to AppData, launch Kenshi via Steam. .mod contains 16 Player CHARACTER GameData templates for remote player spawning.
rawFindings.wiringStatus: Fully wired. Plugins_x64.cfg + Ogre at startup. client.json read at Core init. InstallModFile() called from main:79. SpawnManager discovers templates post-load via FindModTemplates() scanning for Player 1-16 GameData entries.

brokenForSync.symptom: kenshi-online.mod is pre-compiled FCS binary, not generated
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: Created with FCS editor, checked in. No build step. User needs FCS GUI to author fresh mod.

brokenForSync.symptom: ModTemplate selection uses numeric ID at offset 0x08 to pick character vs faction
brokenForSync.suspectedCause: spawn_manager sorts by numId, picks highest. Wrong IDs cause wrong template selection.

duplicatesOrOverlap: install.bat duplicates Injector.exe. Both copy DLL, modify Plugins_x64.cfg. Delete install.bat.
injector.cpp and spawn_manager.cpp both manage .mod: injector deploys, spawn_manager discovers objects.
rawFindings.reorgNote: REFACTOR: (1) Delete install.bat (superseded by Injector.exe). (2) Ship kenshi-online.mod in source root with FCS editor docs.
