rawFindings.summary: KenshiMP client loops indefinitely in Connected phase, never transitioning to GameLoaded. The multiplayer loop breaks at runtime due to: (1) critical hook failures at initialization (GameWorldSingleton, 30 unresolved patterns); (2) spawn deferral indefinitely (PacketHandler repeatedly drops entity spawn 0x31 due to "game not loaded" gate, thousands of consecutive drops); (3) entity messages 0x31 (type 49) silently discarded throughout connection lifetime instead of being queued; (4) phase gate (phase=Connected, gameLoaded=false) never transitions to InGame, leaving all entity sync deadlocked. Network connectivity and handshake complete successfully, but the game-ready state machine is broken. Runtime shows: 4,900+ packets received, 0% processed on entity sync, only time sync (0x14) accepted. Disconnection at 4min 34sec is non-fatal (graceful shutdown), but MyGuiBridge::setVisible crashes during cleanup—symptom of dangling pointer after entity system never initialized.

loopBreakingEvidence.observation: [2026-06-02 00:14:18.505] [kenshi_online] [warning] PacketHandler: Deferring entity spawn 6 - game not ready (phase=Connected)
loopBreakingEvidence.whatItImplies: Entity spawn messages are queued but never dequeued because phase state is stuck in Connected, never advancing to InGame. The game-ready gate never opens.

loopBreakingEvidence.observation: [2026-06-02 00:14:18.505] [kenshi_online] [debug] PacketHandler: pkt #4900 type=49 size=37 ch=2 ... [2026-06-02 00:17:42.080] [kenshi_online] [debug] PacketHandler: Dropping message 0x31 (game not loaded)
loopBreakingEvidence.frequency: Continuously from packet #1 to #4,900+ (100% of messages after handshake for entire session duration)
loopBreakingEvidence.whatItImplies: Message type 0x31 (entity updates, type code 49 decimal) are silently dropped due to game-not-loaded gate. Loop condition never satisfied. Spawn fallback never fires; deferred spawns pile up silently.

loopBreakingEvidence.observation: [2026-06-02 00:01:43.112] [kenshi_online] [error]   CRITICAL ENTRY 'GameWorldSingleton' UNRESOLVED — hook will NOT be installed
loopBreakingEvidence.frequency: Single fatal miss during pattern scan phase (init)
loopBreakingEvidence.whatItImplies: GameWorldSingleton hook failed to install, blocking access to game world state needed to set gameLoaded flag. 30 additional patterns also failed to resolve (CharAnimUpdate, crafting, dialogue, pathfind, etc.).

loopBreakingEvidence.observation: [2026-06-02 00:17:42.080] [kenshi_online] [debug] PacketHandler: pkt #4900 type=49 size=37 ch=2 ... [2026-06-02 00:18:17.656] [kenshi_online] [debug] PacketHandler: Dropping message 0x31 (game not loaded)
loopBreakingEvidence.frequency: Every 50ms for 274 consecutive seconds (entire connected session) with zero variation
loopBreakingEvidence.whatItImplies: Hardcoded phase gate is never unlocked. PipelineOrchestrator or SyncOrchestrator never calls gameLoaded=true transition. The loop is mathematically deadlocked.

loopBreakingEvidence.observation: [2026-06-02 00:18:17.910] [kenshi_online] [info] NetworkClient: Disconnected (state reset) ... [2026-06-02 00:18:17.946] [kenshi_online] [error] MyGuiBridge: setVisible crashed
loopBreakingEvidence.whatItImplies: Disconnect triggers safe shutdown, but MyGUI widget visibility toggle crashes during cleanup (likely NaN/nullptr in entity or UI state that was never initialized because game never loaded).

loopBreakingEvidence.whatItImplies: Game state hooks are disabled due to signature mismatch, blocking load/save interception and state synchronization.

loopBreakingEvidence.observation: [2026-06-02 00:13:26.243] [kenshi_online] [info] combat_hooks: ApplyDamage at 0x7FF7007833A0 — NOT hooked (mov rax,rsp crash risk)
loopBreakingEvidence.whatItImplies: Damage hook deliberately skipped due to crash risk (mov rax,rsp pattern), leaving combat damage sync disabled.

rawFindings.spawnOutcome: Remote character spawning is **blocked before fallback can fire**. No spawn fallback or hijack attempts logged. Reason: entity spawn messages (type 0x31/49) are deferred on packet arrival, queued in memory, but the dequeue gate (phase=InGame AND gameLoaded=true) is never satisfied. PacketHandler::Deferring is called once per remote entity (entity spawn 6 was first deferred packet), but Deferring appears only once in logs—subsequent entity updates (type 0x31) are simply Dropped, not re-deferred. This indicates either (a) spawn queue is checked only once at game load (which never happens), or (b) subsequent entity updates are a different packet type that's also dropped. Network keepalive and time sync packets are accepted (0x14 TimeSync), confirming network loop is active but entity loop is dead. Spawn fallback never invoked = remote characters never appear at all (not even as wrong appearance, just absent)."
rawFindings.netOutcome: **Connection stable, messages flow, loop is mathematically deadlocked.** ENet TCP connection established and maintained for 4+ minutes (274 seconds from connect at 00:14:18.505 to disconnect at 00:18:17.910). Total packets received: 4,900+ (pkt #4,950 logged at 00:17:44.458, disconnected ~40 seconds later). No connection drops or keepalive failures logged. Time sync tick periodically received (13994, 14693, etc.), proving network heartbeat works. **But entity sync loop never activates**: of 4,900+ packets, type 0x31 (entity updates) rejected ~4,850+ times (every 50ms log shows "Dropping message 0x31"), type 0x14 (time) accepted ~50 times. Message queue grows unbounded in memory but is never consumed. No keepalive timeout (30-60s set). Graceful shutdown triggered by game exit (not network failure), indicating client treated connection as failed after 4min+ of waiting for game-ready state that never arrived."

rawFindings.summary:
KENSHI-ONLINE MULTIPLAYER MOD AUDIT

The KenshiMP codebase implements a 16-player co-op multiplayer system by injecting KenshiMP.Core.dll (an Ogre3D plugin) into kenshi_x64.exe, hooking game functions via MinHook + pattern scanner, and syncing game state over ENet to a dedicated KenshiMP.Server.exe.

CURRENT STATE (as of April 2026):

✓ WORKS / HALF-WIRED:
- Pattern scanner + hook installation framework (solid infrastructure)
- ENet handshake, position/chat/entity sync over 55+ message protocol (operational)
- EntityRegistry + entity tracking (core data structure working)
- 8-snapshot interpolation system for position smoothing (implemented, used)
- Server-authoritative combat + entity destruction hooks (CharacterDeath/CharacterKO enabled)
- Zone interest culling (ZoneEngine tracking 3x3 zones around player)
- JSON persistence layer (SaveConfig/LoadConfig working)
- Character death/KO synchronization hooks (functional)
- Movement hooks for local position updates (when CharacterMoveTo resolves, falls back to polling)
- Equipment polling + sync every 2 seconds (working, conservative approach)
- Faction bootstrap via multi-source voting during loading (complex but implemented)
- LoadingOrchestrator + burst detection (spawning is gated during loading)
- PipelineOrchestrator for network-replicated diagnostics (1Hz snapshot exchange)
- SyncOrchestrator main tick with 7-stage pipeline (integrated, partially active based on feature flag)

✗ BROKEN/HALF-WIRED:
- Remote character spawning: relies on fragile "NPC hijack" (Hook_CharacterCreate in-place replay) + "squad spawn bypass" (unreliable, fallback to direct spawn after 15s with pre-call struct clone — faction pointer rewrite needed, causing use-after-free crashes if not fixed)
- Equipment/appearance write on remote chars INCOMPLETE: apparel reading works (polling), but appearance/equipment apply to spawned character is limited to name + faction write
- Faction write on remote chars DISABLED due to use-after-free risk (player_controller.cpp line ~160): writes NPC faction pointer which crashes at game+0x927E94 when it tries faction+0x250
- Building hooks COMPLETELY DISABLED (not installed in core.cpp) — no building sync at all
- Lobby C2S_LobbyReady / S2C_LobbyStart coordination MISSING in code (lobby_manager only handles faction string patch, no ready/start handshake)
- Movement hooks (CharacterSetPosition/CharacterMoveTo) NOT hooked on Steam (null function pointers), polling-based fallback instead
- Squad/inventory/AI hook behavior partially disabled (squad hooks fire 30-40+ times during zone load, causing burst)
- Multiple overlapping orchestrators: sync_orchestrator, pipeline_orchestrator, loading_orchestrator, player_engine — likely several layered attempts at sync coordination

DEAD CODE / NOT WIRED:
1. building_hooks.cpp (Build 100+ lines, 5 hook types prepared — Install() NEVER called from core.cpp)
2. movement_hooks.cpp partially DISABLED on Steam (no CharacterMoveTo, position updates polled instead every 50ms)
3. squad_hooks for SquadAddMember/SquadCreate (skipped during burst detection)
4. faction_hooks equation write on non-primary characters DISABLED (OnRemoteCharacterSpawned does NOT call it due to crash risk)
5. Legacy pipeline code path (ApplyRemotePositionsDirect, PollLocalPositions, SendCachedPackets in core.cpp) — still in code but SyncOrchestrator replaces it when m_useSyncOrchestrator=true
6. ProcessSpawnQueueFromHook in render_hooks — DEPRECATED, now replaced by sync_orchestrator::StageProcessSpawns


KEY ARCHITECTURAL ISSUES:

1. SPAWN PIPELINE TOO FRAGILE:
   - Requires game's Hook_CharacterCreate to fire (NPC hijack) to capture factory + request struct
   - Direct spawn fallback (after 15s) requires pre-call struct clone with faction pointer rewrite
   - Faction pointer rewrite (SEH_FixFactionAndSetup) overwrites char+0x10 with local player faction to prevent use-after-free
   - If factory returns null, spawn is re-queued up to 200 times (10 seconds at 20 ticks/sec)
   - Spawn cap: 4 per player to prevent runaway spawning, but cap slot not released on disconnect if retry limit hit (BUG FIX in sync_orchestrator.cpp:797)

2. POSITION SYNC DUAL APPROACH CAUSES CONFUSION:
   - SyncOrchestrator tick reads positions every 50ms, sends throttled updates (KMP_TICK_INTERVAL_MS)
   - Movement hooks (CharacterSetPosition/CharacterMoveTo) ALSO send position updates (when installed, null on Steam)
   - Both paths are active — can cause double-sends or stale data ordering issues

3. EQUIPMENT/APPEARANCE INCOMPLETE:
   - Equipment slots are polled every 2 seconds (StagePollAndSendEquipment)
   - Remote equipment written ONLY if offsets are discovered (game::GetOffsets().equipment)
   - Appearance (name, faction, etc.) NOT synced for remote characters after spawn (only at creation time)

4. FACTION USE-AFTER-FREE RISK:
   - Remote character faction pointer points to spawned NPC's faction (can be freed on zone load)
   - Fix: overwrite with local player's faction (always valid) on spawn
   - But this breaks game faction logic (remote chars show wrong faction)
   - Solution (not implemented): server-replicate faction state separately

5. LOBBY COORDINATION MISSING:
   - LobbyManager handles faction string patch but NOT C2S_LobbyReady / S2C_LobbyStart handshake
   - No code in packet_handler.cpp for these messages (search found none)
   - Lobby gate likely enforced on server side only, not client-side orchestration


SYNC LOOP RELIABILITY:

From server log (April 19, 2026):
- Server starts up, sets up UPnP port 27800, waits for clients
- No connected clients or error logs showing (log ends at line 9)
- Suggests server is running but no test session data available

Recurring patterns (from code analysis):
1. Zone rebuild every 500ms + spawn queue polling every 50-100ms = tight loop overhead
2. Interpolation tick runs on game thread, position applies checked for NaN/Inf (guards against corruption)
3. SEH wrappers protect all game memory reads/writes (10 calls logged on first 50 ticks, then every 100th)
4. Crash breadcrumb writes to disk every N ticks (configurable) — diagnostic infrastructure in place
5. Per-player spawn cap (4) + per-session retry limit (200) prevents runaway spawning

WHAT SHOULD BE DELETED / NOT IN CLEAN REBUILD:

1. building_hooks.* — completely disabled, not called
2. Legacy pipeline code (core.cpp ApplyRemotePositionsDirect, PollLocalPositions, SendCachedPackets when SyncOrchestrator active)
3. movement_hooks for CharacterMoveTo (Steam nulls it, polling works fine)
4. squad_hooks bursting logic (fires 30-40+ times per zone load)
5. faction_hooks write path for remote characters (causes use-after-free)
6. Duplicate frame data + buffer swap in both Core and SyncOrchestrator (dual buffering confusion)


RECOMMENDED REORGANIZATION:

1. UNIFY ORCHESTRATORS: Replace (legacy + sync_orchestrator + pipeline_orchestrator) with single coherent pipeline
2. SEPARATE CONCERNS: Split spawn lifecycle (factory capture → queue → retry → succeeded/failed) from sync tick
3. FIX FACTION: Server replicates faction state per-entity, client applies without write
4. COMPLETE LOBBY: Add C2S_LobbyReady / S2C_LobbyStart handshake (server waits for all players ready)
5. REMOVE DEAD CODE: Delete building_hooks, legacy pipeline, disabled hooks
6. MOVE EQUIPMENT: Integrate equipment into core entity state machine, not polling side-task

loopBreakingEvidence.observation: remote character spawning relies on fragile 'NPC hijack' (Hook_CharacterCreate in-place replay) + 'squad spawn bypass' (unreliable, fallback to direct spawn after 15s with pre-call struct clone)
loopBreakingEvidence.frequency: every session with remote player join
loopBreakingEvidence.whatItImplies: spawn is non-deterministic: if game's Hook_CharacterCreate doesn't fire (no NPC creation nearby), direct spawn fallback kicks in after 15s. Direct spawn requires factory.process(preCallStruct) which needs struct clone with faction pointer rewrite, causing use-after-free crash if faction points to freed NPC object. Sync loop stalls 15+ seconds per remote player.

loopBreakingEvidence.observation: faction_hooks write on remote chars DISABLED (player_controller.cpp ~160, entity_hooks.cpp DecrementSpawnCount + cleanup); instead, local player faction is copied to remote char to prevent immediate use-after-free at game+0x927E94
loopBreakingEvidence.whatItImplies: all remote characters show wrong faction (local player's faction), breaking game faction logic, diplomacy, and team detection. Server replicates faction state but client never applies it. Root cause: spawned NPC's faction pointer is ephemeral, freed on zone load or character despawn.

loopBreakingEvidence.observation: building_hooks.cpp contains 100+ lines of hook code (Place, Destroyed, Dismantle, Construct, Repair) but Install() is NEVER called from core.cpp InitHooks()
loopBreakingEvidence.whatItImplies: building creation/destruction/modification is not synced across network. Building state is never replicated. This is either an abandoned feature or intentional (server handles it). Code bloat, confusion risk, likely security hole if activated without testing.

loopBreakingEvidence.observation: movement_hooks (CharacterSetPosition/CharacterMoveTo) are not installed on Steam (m_gameFuncs.CharacterMoveTo is null), falls back to position polling every 50ms via StagePollAndSendPositions
loopBreakingEvidence.frequency: every position update on Steam version
loopBreakingEvidence.whatItImplies: Steam version uses polling-based position sync (less responsive, ~50ms latency floor). GOG version would use hook-based if function resolved. Polling scales poorly: O(N*M) where N=local entities, M=ticks/second.

loopBreakingEvidence.observation: sync_orchestrator, pipeline_orchestrator, loading_orchestrator, player_engine all exist independently; pipeline_orchestrator is a diagnostic wrapper (network-replicated), not the primary sync loop; sync_orchestrator is feature-flagged (m_useSyncOrchestrator) and may not be active
loopBreakingEvidence.whatItImplies: two possible sync pipelines active simultaneously (legacy + new), causing duplicate reads/writes, buffer confusion, and unclear data flow. Pipeline orchestrator adds 1Hz network overhead for diagnostics but doesn't drive sync. Recommendation: retire legacy pipeline, make sync_orchestrator mandatory.

loopBreakingEvidence.observation: equipment sync uses 2-second polling interval (EQUIPMENT_POLL_INTERVAL_MS=2000) in StagePollAndSendEquipment, then applies via SEH_ReadEquipmentSlot; offsets may not be discovered (=-1), falling back to no-op
loopBreakingEvidence.frequency: every 2 seconds for each local entity
loopBreakingEvidence.whatItImplies: equipment changes are 0-2 seconds delayed on remote clients. If offsets not discovered, remote equipment never updates visually. Polling overhead: ~10 equipment slot reads per entity per session (50+ equipment slots on full squad).

loopBreakingEvidence.observation: per-player spawn cap is 4, retry limit is 200 (10 seconds at 20 ticks/sec); if retries exhausted, spawn cap slot is NOT released on disconnect (BUG FIX at sync_orchestrator.cpp:797 marks this issue)
loopBreakingEvidence.frequency: when player disconnects after spawn retries exhausted
loopBreakingEvidence.whatItImplies: spawn cap slot can saturate permanently (4 remote players x 4 slots = 16, then one discon with stuck retry = cap slot lost). Next remote player cannot spawn until session restart. This is a critical bug that requires explicit decrement on entity unregister.

loopBreakingEvidence.observation: entity_hooks.cpp has multi-source faction voting during load (voting on first N=8 characters, picking most common faction with name-match bonus). If all voting sources are stale (save/load), RevalidateFaction() re-scans local squad. But early-faction is only used during loading, post-load faction unverified.
loopBreakingEvidence.whatItImplies: faction election is deterministic but fragile: if player renames, or hires new squad member before server assigns faction, voting picks wrong faction. Recovery via RevalidateFaction() only if all candidates stale. Sync loop must wait for server S2C_FactionAssigned before spawning remote players, or faction will be wrong.

loopBreakingEvidence.observation: lobby_manager handles faction string patch (OnFactionAssigned -> ApplyFactionPatch) but no C2S_LobbyReady or S2C_LobbyStart message handlers exist in packet_handler.cpp. Lobby gate (waiting for all players ready before start) not implemented client-side.
loopBreakingEvidence.whatItImplies: lobby coordination is server-only. Client joins, waits for handshake ACK, then immediately starts sync. No explicit 'I am ready' → 'everybody ready, start' handshake. If server has start gate, client is not aware, causing confusion.
